                                              • 186

                                              The Constitution Commission set up by Ayub Khan in 1962 was headed by ______?

                                              Correct Answer: Justice Shahab-ud-Din
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              The Constitution Commission set up by Ayub Khan in 1960-1961


                                              Ayub Khan established the Constitution Commission in 1960 to draft a new constitution.


                                              It was headed by Justice Shahab-ud-Din, a former Chief Justice of Pakistan.


                                              The commission was appointed on February 17, 1960, and presented its report to President Ayub Khan on May 6, 1961. 

                                              187

                                              Ahmed Shah Abdali was defeated by the Mughals in 1748 in which region?

                                              Correct Answer: Sirhind (Punjab)
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Ahmed Shah Abdali was defeated by the Mughals in March 1748


                                              At the Battle of Manupur


                                              Location: Manupur, near Sirhind


                                              Context: First invasion of India by Ahmad Shah Durrani


                                              Mughal Leaders: Prince Ahmad Shah Bahadur, Qamar-ud-Din Khan, and Mir Mannu

                                              188

                                              The first Round Table Conference was held in which city?

                                              Correct Answer: London
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              The First Round Table Conference was held in London


                                              When: November 12, 1930, to January 1931.


                                              Inaugurated by: King George V.


                                              Chaired by: British Prime Minister Ramsay MacDonald.


                                              The Second Round table conference was held from 7 September 1931 to 1 December 1931 in London. 

                                              ****

                                              The Third Round Table Conference was held in 1932 between November and December. 


                                              ****


                                              189

                                              Quit India Movement also called _____?

                                              Correct Answer: August Movement
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              The Quit India Movement is also called the August Movement or August Kranti (August Revolution). 


                                               launched by Mahatma Gandhi on August 8, 1942,in Bombay.


                                              The movement, started by the Congress party.


                                              It's demanded an end to British rule in India.


                                              Muhammad Ali Jinnah responded with the slogan "Divide and Quit India."

                                              190

                                              The book "Ilam-ul-Iqtisad" was authored by:

                                              Correct Answer: Allama Iqbal
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              "Ilam-ul-Iqtisad" was the first book of Allama Muhammad Iqbal,


                                              Published in 1903.


                                              191

                                              Baba Fareed Ganj Shakar belongs to _____?

                                              Correct Answer: Chishti
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Baba Fareed Ganj Shakar belongs to the Chishti Order (Chishtiyya) of Sufism


                                              Baba Farīd or Fariduddin Masud was born in 1188 (573 AH) in Kothewal


                                              He was one of the founding fathers of the Chishti Sufi order.


                                              The Shrine is located in Pakpattan, Pakistan


                                              Baba Fareed Shaker Ganj' was the first poet of Punjabi


                                              192

                                              Who was the first person in Pakistan who received Nishan-i-Haider?

                                              Correct Answer: Captain Sarwar Shaheed
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Nishan e Haider has been given 11 soldiers since Pakistan's independence in 1947.

                                              1. Captain Muhammad Sarwar (1948)
                                              2. Sardar Muhammad Hussain Khan (1948)
                                              3. Major Tufail Muhammad (1958)
                                              4. Major Aziz Bhatti (1965)
                                              5. Major Rafiq Bokhari (1971)
                                              6. Lieutenant Colonel Abdul Hafeez (1971)
                                              7. Lance Naik Muhammad Mahfuz (1971)
                                              8. Naik Saif Ali Janjua (1971)
                                              9. Major Shabbir Sharif (1971)
                                              10. Captain Karnal Sher Khan (1999)
                                              11. Lance Naik Muhammad Imran (1999)
                                              193

                                              Pakistan Ideology is based on the idea of ____?

                                              Correct Answer: Islam
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Pakistan's ideology is fundamentally based on Islam and the Two-Nation Theory


                                              Islamic Identity: The belief that Islam is a complete way of life

                                              Two-Nation Theory: Muslims and Hindus are two separate nations


                                              194

                                              Pakistan sold 4.6 billion dollars worth of JF-17 aircraft to which country?

                                              Correct Answer: Azerbaijan
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Pakistan has sold 40 JF-17 Thunder Block III aircraft to Azerbaijan


                                              The total deal is worth around $4.6 billion,


                                               This is considered the largest export contract in Pakistan's defense industry history. 

                                              195

                                              In which year was GSP+ status given to Pakistan by the EU?

                                              Correct Answer: 2018
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Pakistan was granted GSP+ status by the European Union (EU) on January 1, 2014

                                               

                                              After the country had ratified 27 international conventions


                                              GSP+ (Generalised Scheme of Preferences Plus) 


                                               It was approved in late 2013


                                              196

                                              What is the primary industry along the coastline of Balochistan and Sindh?

                                              Correct Answer: Fishing
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              The primary industry along the coastline of Balochistan and Sindh is fisheries. 


                                              The fishing industry plays a significant part in the national economy of Pakistan.


                                              With a coastline of about 650 miles (1,046 km), 

                                              197

                                              Pakistan’s hyperspectral satellite HS-1 is mainly launched for what purpose?

                                              Correct Answer: Land monitoring and agriculture
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Pakistan’s first hyperspectral satellite, HS-1, is primarily designed for Land monitoring and agriculture.

                                               

                                              launched in October 2025


                                              Developed by SUPARCO

                                              198

                                              Which book was not written by Faiz Ahmad Faiz? a) Dard Ashoob, b) Naqsh-e-Faryadi, c) Zindan-Nama, d) Dast-e-Saba

                                              Correct Answer: Dard Ashoob
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              The book that was not written by Faiz Ahmad Faiz is Dard Ashoob. 

                                              Dard Ashoob is a book of poetry written by Ahmad Faraz.


                                              Additional information 

                                              • Naqsh-e-Faryadi (1941) by Faiz Ahmad Faiz.
                                              • Zindan-Nama (1956) was written by Faiz Ahmad Faiz 
                                              • Dast-e-Saba (1952) was written by Faiz Ahmad Faiz, largely during his confinement in Hyderabad jail. 
                                              199

                                              Awaz-e-Dost was written by whom?

                                              Correct Answer: Mukhtar Masood
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Awaz-e-Dost was written by Mukhtar Masood


                                               Published in 1973


                                              Theme: The book covers the Pakistan movement, specifically focusing on its history and events.

                                              200

                                              Per Capita water availability in Pakistan is _____?

                                              Correct Answer: Decreasing
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • Per capita water availability in Pakistan is decreasing by 400pc.
                                              • Due to population growth and diminishing water resources.
                                              • This leading to increased concerns over water scarcity.

                                              Last verified on 27-01-2026

                                              201

                                              Which Pakistani female cricketer was inducted into the Hall of Fame in 2025.?

                                              Correct Answer: Sana Mir
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Sana Mir is the first Pakistani Woman in the ICC Hall of Fame: Inducted in 2025.


                                              ODI wickets (151) by a Pakistani woman.


                                              First Pakistani woman to top the ICC ODI bowling rankings in 2018.


                                              Born: 5 January 1986, Abbottabad, KPK



                                              202

                                              What is the current Tax to GDP ratio of Pakistan?

                                              Correct Answer: 11%
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Pakistan's tax-to-GDP ratio for the fiscal year 2024-25 (ending June 2025) has increased to approximately 10.3%–10.6%


                                              Target: 11% for the current fiscal year.


                                              Goal: 18% by 2027-28.

                                              203

                                              PIA was purchased by whom?

                                              Correct Answer: Arif Habib Consortium
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              A consortium led by the Arif Habib Group


                                              Stake Purchased: 75% of PIA.


                                              Transaction Amount: Rs135 billion (approximately $482 million)  in December 2025. 



                                              Additional information related PIA

                                              Pakistan International Airlines is an international airline of Pakistan

                                              Headquarters: Karachi

                                              Founded: October 29, 1946

                                              Founder: Mirza Ahmad Ispahani

                                              204

                                              Which is a traditional musical instrument from Pakistan?

                                              Correct Answer: Alghoza
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Traditional instruments from Pakistan include the :

                                              • Rabab (a plucked string instrument from the north),
                                              • Alghoza (paired flutes),
                                              • Dhol (drum),
                                              • Harmonium,
                                              • Tabla.


                                              The Been (also known as Murli or Pungi) is a wind instrument used by snake charmers

                                              205

                                              What is a common source of protein in the Pakistani diet?

                                              Correct Answer: Meat
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Meat is a primary source of protein in the traditional Pakistani diet.


                                              Common types include chicken, beef, mutton, and fish.

                                              206

                                              In 2025, Pakistan Under-19s won by 191 runs against India. Who scored 172 runs in that match?

                                              Correct Answer: Sameer Minhas
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              In 2025, the Pakistan Under-19 cricket team defeated India by 191 runs.


                                              Sameer Minhas played a brilliant innings,


                                              Record-breaking 172, Pakistan posted a daunting 347 for eight before dismissing India for 156 in just 26.2 overs. 

                                              207

                                              The constitution of Pakistan 1973. can be amended______

                                              Correct Answer: With two-third majority of both the houses of partiament.
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Approval Requirement: The bill must pass with a two-thirds majority of the total membership in both the National Assembly and the Senate.


                                              At the age of 21 years, the charter, that changed into adopted in April 1973, 

                                              Has suffered 34 amendments; 10 times through the Acts of Parliament and 24 times through Presidential Orders


                                              .Amendments to Date: As of late 2024, the Constitution has been amended 26 times. 





                                              208

                                              on which river "Dasu Dam" is being constructed?

                                              Correct Answer: River Indus
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              The Dasu Hydropower Project is located on the Indus River, 7km upstream of Dasu Town,

                                              District Kohistan, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KPK).

                                              Names Dam Location  River completed
                                              Rawal Dam Islamabad  Korang River 1962
                                              Mangla Dam Mirpur District - AJK Jhelum River 1967
                                              Tarbela Dam Haripur - KPK Indus River 1974
                                              Hub Dam Lasbela Districts Sindh Hub River 1979
                                              Khanpur Dam Haripur - KPK Haro River 1985
                                              Diamer Bhasha Dam Gilgit Baltistan - KPK Indus River
                                              Dasu Dam  Kohistan District - KPK Indus River

                                              209

                                              What caused the Pakistan-India tension in 2025?

                                              Correct Answer: Water sharing issue
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              On 22 April 2025, gunmen attacked tourists in Pahalgam,


                                              At least 24 people were killed in India-occupied Kashmir when gunmen opened fire on a group of tourists.


                                              Additional Information: 

                                              After this attack, India suspended the Indus Waters Treaty 

                                              Closed Check-post named Attari 

                                              Asked Pakistani nationals to leave India within 48 Hours 

                                              210

                                              Who won the KC Lightweight Championship in 2025?

                                              Correct Answer: Shahzaib Rind
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Shahzaib Rind becomes KC Lightweight World Champion


                                              In 2025, at Karate Combat KC 56.


                                              He defeated Luis Rocha to claim the title and extend his undefeated record.


                                              Rind had previously defended his lightweight title at KC 52 in Miami.

                                              211

                                              Which one was the first Urdu Film of Pakistan?

                                              Correct Answer: Teri Yaad
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Teri Yaad was a Pakistani Urdu-language film


                                              Released on 7 August 1948.


                                              Teri Yaad was the first film released after the independence of Pakistan on 14 August 1947.

                                              212

                                              The rift between India and Pakistan started after which event?

                                              Correct Answer: Partition of 1947
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              The rift between India and Pakistan originated after the Partition of British India in August 1947 


                                              It was solidified by the subsequent First Indo-Pakistani War (1947–1948)


                                              The war ended on January 1, 1949, with a UN-brokered ceasefire


                                              Divided Kashmir, with India holding roughly two-thirds of the territory and Pakistan one-third.

                                              213

                                              The World Bank gave Pakistan how much for macroeconomic stability?

                                              Correct Answer: 700 billion
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              On December 19-20, 2025, the World Bank approved $700 million 


                                              In financing to support Pakistan’s macroeconomic stability


                                              Objective: To improve domestic revenue mobilization, strengthen fiscal systems, and enhance service delivery.

                                              214

                                              Amir Khan is famous for which sport?

                                              Correct Answer: Boxing
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Amir Khan is a British-Pakistani professional boxer.


                                              He is the founder of the Super Boxing League. 


                                              He won a silver medal in the lightweight division at the 2004 Athens Olympics at the age of 17


                                              Held the WBA (later Super) title from 2009 to 2012, and the IBF title in 2011.


                                              Competed from 2005 to 2022.

                                              215

                                              The 27th Amendment of the Pakistan Constitution is related to?

                                              Correct Answer: Constitutional Court
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              The 27th Amendment to the Constitution of Pakistan, approved in November 2025


                                              Deals with the establishment and powers of the Constitutional Court.


                                              It clarifies the jurisdiction and authority of the court in interpreting the Constitution.

                                              216

                                              Which Pakistani cricketer played in 6 consecutive World Cups?

                                              Correct Answer: Javed Miandad
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Javed Miandad is one of Pakistan’s legendary cricketers.


                                              He played in six consecutive Cricket World Cups from 1975 to 1996.


                                              Additional information 

                                              • Editions: 1975, 1979, 1983, 1987, 1992, 1996.
                                              • Total Matches: 33.
                                              • Total Runs: 1,083.
                                              • Achievement: Member of the 1992 World Cup-winning team. 
                                              217

                                              Dul Hasti Stage II by India is built on which river?

                                              Correct Answer: Chenab River
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              The Dul Hasti Stage II hydropower project is built by India on the Chenab River 

                                              Location: Kishtwar district, Jammu and Kashmir, India.

                                              Approved in December 2025

                                              Capacity: 260 MW.

                                              Developer: NHPC Limited.

                                              218

                                              Pakistan launched its first indigenous electro-optical satellite from which country?

                                              Correct Answer: China
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Pakistan launched its first, entirely domestically designed satellite from the Jiuquan Satellite Launch Centre in China


                                              Satellite Name: PRSC-EO1 (or EO-1)


                                              Date: January 17, 2025


                                              Developer: Pakistan Space and Upper Atmosphere Research Commission (SUPARCO)


                                              Purpose: To monitor natural resources, support food security, and assist in urban planning 

                                               ***

                                              219

                                              The Shandur Polo Festival is usually held every year on?

                                              Correct Answer: July 7–9
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              The Shandur Polo Festival is usually held every year from July 7 to July 9. 


                                              It is a sports festival heldat the Shandur Polo Ground in Pakistan


                                              Located at the boundary of the Gupis-Yasin and Chitral Districts.


                                              Played between the teams of Gilgit Baltistan and Chitral, 


                                              Established 1930


                                              Founder: Colonel Evelyn Hey Cobb

                                              220

                                              APTMA stands for ______?

                                              Correct Answer: All Pakistan Textile Mills Association
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • APTMA officially stands for All Pakistan Textile Mills Association.
                                              • It represents spinning, weaving, processing, and composite textile mills.

                                                Additional information:

                                                • Founded: 1958
                                                • Role: Protects interests of textile industry and promotes exports

                                                Last verified on 25-01-2026

                                                221

                                                Which act introduced Dyarchy in the provinces?

                                                Correct Answer: Government of India Act 1919
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation
                                                • Diarchy = system of shared rule in provinces under British India.
                                                  • Introduced by the Government of India Act 1919.
                                                  • Meaning: “Double system of government” – two heads responsible for administration.
                                                  • Introduced by Edwin Samuel Montagu and Lord Chelmsford.
                                                  • Ended by Government of India Act 1935, which established provincial autonomy.

                                                  Last verified on 23-01-2026

                                                  222

                                                  Pakistan came into existence in which year?

                                                  Correct Answer: 1947
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation

                                                  Pakistan came into existence on August 14, 1947


                                                  27th of Ramadan in 1366 of the Islamic Calendar, Pakistan gained independence.


                                                  223

                                                  The book “The Songs of Blood and Sword” is written by ______?

                                                  Correct Answer: Fatima Bhutto
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation
                                                  “The Songs of Blood and Sword” is an autobiographical book written by Fatima Bhutto.
                                                  Additional information:
                                                  • Published in 2010.
                                                  • She is the daughter of Murtaza Bhutto.
                                                  • Covers events from Bhutto family history (1970s–2000s).
                                                  • Discusses the assassination of her father in 1996.
                                                  • Provides insights into Pakistan’s political turmoil during the Bhutto era.

                                                  Last verified on 22-01-2026

                                                  224

                                                  The Punjabi translation of Bal‑e‑Jibrel by Allama Iqbal was done by ______?

                                                  Correct Answer: Aseer Abid
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation
                                                  • Bal-e-Jibreel is a famous work by Allama Iqbal, originally in Urdu.
                                                  • Its Punjabi translation, titled “Jibreel di Udari”, was done by Aseer Abid.
                                                  • Published by the Iqbal Academy Pakistan in 1995.
                                                  • Includes ghazals, poems, and rubaiyat, keeping Iqbal’s original themes of love and philosophy.

                                                  Last verified on 22-01-2026

                                                  225

                                                  The Pakistani surgeon imprisoned in an American jail is ______?

                                                  Correct Answer: Aafia Siddiqui
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation
                                                  • Full Name: Dr. Aafia Siddiqui
                                                  • Arrested: 2008 (Afghanistan)
                                                  • Sentence: 86 years (2010)
                                                  • Prison: FMC Carswell, Texas (USA)
                                                  • Education: MIT (USA), Brandeis University

                                                  Last verified on 22-01-2026

                                                  226

                                                  Who is the current leader of the opposition in the Senate of Pakistan?

                                                  Correct Answer: Raja Nasir Abbas
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation

                                                  Raja Nasir Abbas is the current leader of the opposition in the Senate of Pakistan since 2026.

                                                  He is the chief of Majlis Wahdat-i-Muslimeen (MWM).

                                                  He replaced Shibli Faraz who was disqualified in August 2025.


                                                  Last Updated on 20-01-2026.

                                                  227

                                                  Sythet District at the time of partition was part of province of?

                                                  Correct Answer: Assam
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation
                                                  At the time of India's Partition in 1947, the Sylhet District was part of the Assam Province

                                                  It is the northeastern division of Bangladesh.  

                                                  It was formed from East Pakistan after its independence in 1971

                                                  The Sylhet Division is named after its headquarters, the city of Sylhet.
                                                  228

                                                  Which article of the 1973 Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan prohibits all forms of slavery, forced labor and child labor?

                                                  Correct Answer: 11
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation

                                                  Article 11 of the 1973 Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan prohibits all forms of slavery, forced labor, and child labor. 


                                                  Article 11(1) declares that slavery is non-existent and forbidden.


                                                  Article 11(2) prohibits all forms of forced labor and human trafficking.


                                                  Article 11(3) prohibits the engagement of children below the age of fourteen years in any factory, mine, or hazardous employment. 

                                                  229

                                                  The Chief Election Commissioner have tenure of _______?

                                                  Correct Answer: 5 years
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation
                                                  • CEC Tenure: 5 years
                                                    • Election Commission Members: 4
                                                    • Total Members (ECP): 5
                                                    • Constitutional Article: 215
                                                    • Retirement Age: No early removal except through Supreme Judicial Council

                                                    Last Updated on 13-01-2026

                                                    230

                                                    Iblees ki Majlis-e-Shura is a poem written by _____?

                                                    Correct Answer: Allama Iqbal
                                                    Show explanation
                                                    Explanation
                                                    • “Iblees ki Majlis-e-Shura” is a satirical poem by Allama Iqbal.
                                                      • Critiques the social and political situation of his time.
                                                      • Portrays a fictional council meeting of Iblees (Satan).
                                                      • Offers insightful reflections on human nature and society.
                                                      • Written in Urdu, famous for wit and satire.

                                                      Last verified on 20-01-2026

                                                      231

                                                      According to Mehmood Sherani, the first Urdu poet was _____?

                                                      Correct Answer: Syed Salman Masood
                                                      Show explanation
                                                      Explanation

                                                      First Urdu Poet According to Hafiz Mehmood Sherani

                                                      • Poet: Saad Salman (Mas'ud Sa'd Salman)
                                                      • Reference: Punjab Mein Urdu by Hafiz Mehmood Sherani
                                                      • Argument: Urdu developed in Punjab after Mahmud of Ghazni’s conquest
                                                      • Language Origin: Fusion of local dialects + Persian + Arabic
                                                      • Earlier Views: Muhammad Hussain Azad considered Wali Dakhni as first Urdu poet
                                                      • Sherani’s Conclusion: First Urdu poet was Mas'ud Sa'd Salman from Lahore

                                                      Last verified on 20-01-2026

                                                      232

                                                      Who was the First Poet of the Urdu language?

                                                      Correct Answer: Amir Khusrow
                                                      Show explanation
                                                      Explanation
                                                      • Ameer Khusro is considered the first poet of Pashto and Urdu.
                                                      • Credited with invention of the sitar
                                                      • Wrote ghazals, qawwalis, and riddles

                                                        Additional information:

                                                        • Full Name: Ab'ul Hasan Yamīn ud-Dīn Khusrau
                                                          Born: 1253, Patiyali, India
                                                          Died: October 1325, Delhi, India
                                                          Burial Place: Hazrat Nizamuddin Aulia Dargah, New Delhi, India

                                                          Other Related Poets:

                                                          • Baba Farid: First Punjabi poet, known for Sufi poetry.
                                                          • Allama Iqbal: National poet of Pakistan, philosopher and visionary.
                                                          • Hassan Bin Sabit: First poet of Islam, composed poetry praising Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).

                                                          Last verified on 20-01-2026

                                                          233

                                                          Under the Factories Act, 1934 no adult employee... can be required... to work... in excess of ____ hours a day and ____ hours a week.

                                                          Correct Answer: 9, 48
                                                          Show explanation
                                                          Explanation

                                                          the Factories Act, 1934, in Pakistan, sets standard working hours for adults at 9 hours daily and 48 hours weekly


                                                          Seasonal Factories: Up to 9 hours/day, 50 hours/week.


                                                          Continuous Work: Up to 56 hours/week (with exceptions).


                                                          Overtime: Double the ordinary rate, capped at 150 hours/quarter. 

                                                          234

                                                          What is Pakistan's biggest export commodity?

                                                          Correct Answer: Textiles
                                                          Show explanation
                                                          Explanation
                                                          • Textiles are Pakistan’s largest export commodity.
                                                          • The textile sector plays a key role in Pakistan’s economy and foreign exchange earnings.

                                                          Additional information:
                                                          Major textile export items:

                                                          • Cotton yarn
                                                          • Readymade garments
                                                          • Bed wear
                                                          • Towels
                                                          • Knitwear

                                                          Last verified on 19-01-2026

                                                          235

                                                          F. E. Chaudhary was the ______ of Pakistan.

                                                          Correct Answer: Photographer
                                                          Show explanation
                                                          Explanation
                                                          • Faustin Elmer Chaudhary, popularly known as “Chacha”.
                                                          • He was a pioneering photographer and photojournalist in Pakistan.
                                                          • Known as the father of news photography in Pakistan.

                                                          Additional information:

                                                          Major contributions:

                                                          • 1947 Partition – documented events as the only local photographer
                                                          • 1940 Muslim League Session in Lahore
                                                          • Visits of Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah
                                                          • Worked at The Pakistan Times from 1949 to 1973
                                                          • Received awards: Pride of Performance, Sitara-i-Imtiaz, Tamgha-i-Khidmat

                                                          Last verified on 19-01-2026

                                                          236

                                                          Which sector is the largest contributor to Pakistan's GDP?

                                                          Correct Answer: Services
                                                          Show explanation
                                                          Explanation
                                                          • The services sector is the largest contributor to Pakistan’s GDP.
                                                          • It accounting for around 58% of the total GDP.
                                                          • Agriculture contributes about 23%.
                                                          • Industry/manufacturing contributes around 20%.

                                                            Additional information:

                                                            • Services include finance, transport, education, health, and IT.
                                                            • Growth in services reflects urbanization and modernization of the economy.

                                                            Last verified on 19-01-2026

                                                            237

                                                            The Lucknow Pact of 1916 was made between ______?

                                                            Correct Answer: The Congress and the Muslim League
                                                            Show explanation
                                                            Explanation
                                                            Lucknow Pact (1916) was an agreement between:
                                                            • Indian National Congress – headed by Bal Gangadhar Tilak
                                                            • All-India Muslim League – led by Muhammad Ali Jinnah
                                                            Lucknow Pact Purposes
                                                            • Hindu-Muslim unity
                                                            • Congress-League cooperation
                                                            • Constitutional reforms
                                                            • Separate electorates for Muslims
                                                            • Demand for self-government

                                                            Last verified on 19-01-2026

                                                            238

                                                            Pakistan’s major export earnings come from ______?

                                                            Correct Answer: Cotton
                                                            Show explanation
                                                            Explanation
                                                            • Cotton is Pakistan’s major export commodity.
                                                            • It contributing significantly to foreign exchange earnings.
                                                            • It is used in the textile industry for making fabrics, garments, and yarn.

                                                              Last verified on 19-01-2026

                                                              239

                                                              The national fruit of Pakistan is _____?

                                                              Correct Answer: Mango
                                                              Show explanation
                                                              Explanation

                                                              National fruit of Pakistan: Mango


                                                              Additional information:

                                                              • National Bird: Chukar
                                                              • National Animal: Markhor
                                                              • National Dish: Nihari
                                                              • National Flower: Jasmine
                                                              • National Tree: Himalayan Cedar (Deodar)
                                                              • National Vegetable: Lady Finger (Okra)

                                                              Last verified on 19-01-2026

                                                              240

                                                              Pakistan last census was held in?

                                                              Correct Answer: 2023
                                                              Show explanation
                                                              Explanation

                                                              Pakistan's last census, the 7th Population & Housing Census, was indeed held in 2023  


                                                              from March to May 2023


                                                              Recorded Pakistan's population at 241.5 million (approx.).


                                                              Pakistan's Constitution requires a census every 10 years.

                                                              There are the Total Seven Census held in Pakistan

                                                              1951 (First census)

                                                              1961 (Second census)

                                                              1972 (Third census)

                                                              1981 (Fourth census)

                                                              1998 (Fifth census)

                                                              2017 (Sixth census)

                                                              2023 (Seventh, first digital census) 

                                                              Next 2033

                                                               


                                                              241

                                                              Malakand pass connects?

                                                              Correct Answer: Peshawar with chitral
                                                              Show explanation
                                                              Explanation

                                                              The Malakand Passis a mountain pass in the Malakand District of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa


                                                              Connects Peshawar with the Chitral District.


                                                              Height: 1362 meters, 4468 feet 


                                                              The pass came into prominence in 1895 during the Chitral Expedition, 



                                                              242

                                                              Which is the highest battlefield in the world?

                                                              Correct Answer: The Siachen Glacier
                                                              Show explanation
                                                              Explanation

                                                              The highest battlefield in the world is the Siachen Glacier


                                                              A military dispute between India and Pakistan began on April 13, 1984.


                                                              It has an average altitude of about 6,000 meters (20,000 feet).


                                                              It is also known as the Siachen Glacier conflict.


                                                              It is located in the Karakoram range in Kashmir.


                                                              243

                                                              Cholistan desert is also called _____?

                                                              Correct Answer: Rohi Desert
                                                              Show explanation
                                                              Explanation
                                                              • Cholistan Desert
                                                                • Local Name: Rohi
                                                                • Location: Southern Punjab, Pakistan
                                                                • Distance from Bahawalpur: ~30 km
                                                                • Famous Event: Cholistan Jeep Rally
                                                                • Area Coverage: Approximately 26,000 km²
                                                                Last verified on 18-01-2026
                                                              244

                                                              Constantinople fell into the hands of Muslims in which year?

                                                              Correct Answer: 1453 AD
                                                              Show explanation
                                                              Explanation
                                                              • Date of Conquest: 29 May 1453
                                                                • Siege Duration: 55 days
                                                                • Ottoman Sultan: Mehmed II (Mehmed the Conqueror)
                                                                • Significance: End of Byzantine Empire; start of Ottoman dominance in Eastern Europe
                                                                • Population of Constantinople at Fall: ~50,000 – 80,000

                                                                Last verified on 18-01-2026

                                                                245

                                                                Who was the First Governor of East Bengal after Independence?

                                                                Correct Answer: Frederick Bourne
                                                                Show explanation
                                                                Explanation

                                                                Frederick Chalmers Bourne was the First Governor of East Bengal after Independence.


                                                                Additional information:

                                                                • Tenure: 15 August 1947 – 5 April 1950
                                                                • Nationality: British
                                                                • Province: East Bengal (later East Pakistan, now Bangladesh)
                                                                • Key Role: Managed administrative transition from British India to Pakistan

                                                                Last verified on 18-01-2026

                                                                246

                                                                First underground drainage system was introduced by which civilization?

                                                                Correct Answer: Indus valley civilization
                                                                Show explanation
                                                                Explanation
                                                                The first underground drainage system in history was introduced by the Indus Valley Civilization (IVC).

                                                                Additional information:
                                                                • Time Period: 3300 BCE – 1300 BCE (entire civilization)
                                                                • Mature Phase: 2600–1900 BCE
                                                                • Major Cities with Drainage: Mohenjo-daro, Harappa, Dholavira, Lothal, Kalibangan
                                                                • Number of Main Drains in Mohenjo-daro: Over 700 household drains connected to covered sewers
                                                                • Population Estimate: Around 1–2 million people in urban centers

                                                                Last verified on 18-01-2026

                                                                247

                                                                Who was the First Muslim Musician in sub-continent?

                                                                Correct Answer: Ameer Khusro
                                                                Show explanation
                                                                Explanation

                                                                Ameer Khusro (1253–1325 AD) was a famous Sufi poet, scholar, and musician in the Indian subcontinent.

                                                                He was served under Sultan Alauddin Khilji and later Ghiyasuddin Tughlaq.


                                                                Additional information:

                                                                • Birth Year: 1253 AD
                                                                • Death Year: 1325 AD
                                                                • Also called as: “Tuti-e-Hind” (Parrot of India)

                                                                Last verified on 18-01-2026

                                                                248

                                                                Kavaan elephant was sent from Pakistan to which country on the direction of court?

                                                                Correct Answer: Cambodia
                                                                Show explanation
                                                                Explanation
                                                                • Kavaan, a male elephant, was famous for being kept in poor conditions in Pakistan.
                                                                • Kavaan was kept at Marghazar Zoo, Islamabad before relocation.
                                                                • In 2021, he was sent to Wildlife Friends Foundation Thailand (WFFT) Elephant Sanctuary in Cambodia for rehabilitation.

                                                                Last verified on 18-01-2026

                                                                249

                                                                The Gandhara School of art developed during the reign of the _____?

                                                                Correct Answer: Kushanas
                                                                Show explanation
                                                                Explanation
                                                                • Ruling Dynasty: Kushanas
                                                                • Time Period: 1st century CE – 5th century CE
                                                                • Main Centers: Taxila, Peshawar Valley, Swat Valley
                                                                • Style Influences: Greek (Hellenistic), Roman, Indian
                                                                • Subjects: Buddha statues, Bodhisattvas, stupas

                                                                Last verified on 18-01-2026

                                                                250

                                                                FATF removed Pakistan from grey list after four years on which date?

                                                                Correct Answer: 21 October 2022
                                                                Show explanation
                                                                Explanation

                                                                Financial Action Task Force (FATF)

                                                                • Added to Grey List: June 2018
                                                                  • Removed from Grey List: 21 October 2022
                                                                  • Duration on Grey List: ~4 years
                                                                  • Votes required to exit Grey List: 12 out of 39

                                                                    Additional related updates (2023):

                                                                    • South Africa & Nigeria added to Grey List on 24 February 2023
                                                                    • Cambodia & Morocco removed from Grey List on 24 February 2023
                                                                    251

                                                                    In which year did the Cease Fire Line came into existence?

                                                                    Correct Answer: 1949
                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                    Explanation
                                                                    • Cease Fire Date: 1 January 1949
                                                                    • Based on: Part I of the UN Resolution of 13 August 1948
                                                                    • Purpose: Suspension of hostilities and delineation of control areas
                                                                    • Countries involved: India and Pakistan
                                                                    • Later Name: Line of Control (LoC)

                                                                    Last verified on 18-01-2026

                                                                    252

                                                                    Which country President visited Pakistan in March 2022?

                                                                    Correct Answer: Uzbekistan
                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                    Explanation
                                                                    • Visiting Country: Uzbekistan
                                                                    • President: Shavkat Mirziyoyev
                                                                    • Visit Month and Year: March 2022
                                                                    • Purpose: Trade, investment, and regional cooperation

                                                                    Last verified on 18-01-2026

                                                                    253

                                                                    • Issue: Speaker’s powers limited by ordinance
                                                                    • Issued by: Governor Balighur Rehman
                                                                    • Affected body: Punjab Provincial Assembly

                                                                    254

                                                                    How many general seats are there in the National Assembly of Pakistan?

                                                                    Correct Answer: 266
                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                    Explanation
                                                                    • General Seats: 266
                                                                      • Women Seats: 60
                                                                      • Non-Muslim Seats: 10
                                                                      • Total Seats: 336
                                                                      • Old strength: 342 (reduced after KPK seats changed from 61 → 55)
                                                                      • Constitution Reference: Article 51 (1) & 3(a)

                                                                      Additional information:
                                                                      • Minimum age to contest NA election: 25 years
                                                                      • Seats increase or decrease based on population
                                                                      • National Assembly = Lower House
                                                                      • Senate = Upper House

                                                                      Last verified on 18-01-2026

                                                                      255

                                                                      Jurist Manzur Qadir was Pakistani foreign minister during _____?

                                                                      Correct Answer: 1958 to 1962
                                                                      Show explanation
                                                                      Explanation

                                                                      Manzur Qadir

                                                                      • Office: Foreign Minister of Pakistan
                                                                      • Tenure: 1958 – 1962
                                                                      • President at the time: Muhammad Ayub Khan
                                                                      • Profession: Jurist
                                                                      • Era: Early military-led government

                                                                      Last verified on 18-01-2026

                                                                      • English edition published: 1961
                                                                      • Urdu edition published: 1967

                                                                      257

                                                                      The Stock Exchange of Pakistan is regulated by?

                                                                      Correct Answer: SECP
                                                                      Show explanation
                                                                      Explanation
                                                                      • Regulatory Authority: SECP (Securities and Exchange Commission of Pakistan)
                                                                      • Headquarters: Karachi, Sindh, Pakistan
                                                                      • Formerly: Karachi Stock Exchange Limited
                                                                      • Foundation Date: 18 September 1947 (as Karachi Stock Exchange)
                                                                      • Purpose: Regulate stock market, protect investors, ensure transparency

                                                                      258

                                                                      The cultural centre of ancient Gandhara civilization was situated at _______?

                                                                      Correct Answer: Taxila
                                                                      Show explanation
                                                                      Explanation
                                                                      • Main Cultural Center: Taxila
                                                                      • Location: Peshawar Valley, near present-day Islamabad, Pakistan
                                                                      • Time Period: 6th century BCE – 5th century CE
                                                                      • Purpose: Education, trade, and religious spread (Buddhism)
                                                                      • Connected Regions: South Asia, Central Asia, Middle East

                                                                      Last verified on 18-01-2026

                                                                      259

                                                                      Which Muslim Majority area was given to India to accommodate for access to Kashmir?

                                                                      Correct Answer: Gurdaspur
                                                                      Show explanation
                                                                      Explanation
                                                                      • District: Gurdaspur
                                                                      • Population (1941 Census): Approx. 51% Muslim majority
                                                                      • Year of Partition: 1947
                                                                      • Purpose: Provided India with access to Kashmir
                                                                      • Adjacent districts: Amritsar, Pathankot, Lahore (nearby in Punjab region)

                                                                      Last verified on 18-01-2026

                                                                      260

                                                                      Who said the accession of Kashmir with India is illegal and fraudulent?

                                                                      Correct Answer: Quaid-e-Azam
                                                                      Show explanation
                                                                      Explanation
                                                                      • Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah declared that the accession of Kashmir with India was illegal and fraudulent.
                                                                      • He raised the issue in 1947, soon after independence, demanding that Kashmir be part of Pakistan.
                                                                      • This statement was part of Pakistan’s diplomatic and political stance on the Kashmir dispute.

                                                                      Last verified on 18-01-2026

                                                                      • Issue: Speaker’s powers limited by ordinance

                                                                      • Affected body: Punjab Provincial Assembly

                                                                      • First Muslim Commander-in-Chief: General Muhammad Ayub Khan (1951)

                                                                      Who was the First Governor of West Punjab after independence?

                                                                      Correct Answer: Francis Modie
                                                                      Show explanation

                                                                      First Governor: Sir Francis Mudie (15 Aug 1947 – 2 Aug 1949)


                                                                      Additional information:

                                                                      • West Punjab Province Duration: 15 August 1947 – 14 October 1955
                                                                        • Capital: Lahore
                                                                        • Divisions: Lahore, Sargodha, Multan, Rawalpindi

                                                                        Subsequent Governors:

                                                                          • Sardar Abdur Rab Nishtar (2 Aug 1949 – 24 Nov 1951)
                                                                          • Ismail Ibrahim Chundrigar (24 Nov 1951 – 2 May 1953)
                                                                          • Mian Aminuddin (2 May 1953 – 24 Jun 1954)
                                                                          • Habib Ibrahim Rahmatullah (26 Sep 1954 – 26 Nov 1954)
                                                                          • Mushtaq Ahmad Gurmani (27 Nov 1954 – 14 Oct 1955)

                                                                          Last verified on 18-01-2026

                                                                          • Presidency period: 14 Nov 1993 – 2 Dec 1997

                                                                          • Death: 20 Oct 2010 (aged 70)
                                                                          • Political party: Pakistan Muslim League (Q) 2002–2010
