                                                                          266

                                                                          Who was the First Civilian Chief Martial Law Administrator Pakistan on December 20, 1971?

                                                                          Correct Answer: Zulifqar Ali Bhutto
                                                                          Show explanation
                                                                          Explanation
                                                                          • Full Name: Zulifqar Ali Bhutto
                                                                          • Position: First Civilian Chief Martial Law Administrator & First Civilian President
                                                                          • Date of Appointment: 20 December 1971 (after Yahya Khan resigned)
                                                                          • Political Party: Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP)

                                                                          Additional information:

                                                                          • Iskandar Ali Mirza: First President of Pakistan; abrogated 1956 Constitution on 7 October 1958
                                                                          • Ayub Khan: Made Chief Martial Law Administrator after 1958 abrogation
                                                                          • Feroz Khan Noon: Prime Minister at the time of first martial law in Pakistan (1958)

                                                                          Last verified on 18-01-2026

                                                                          267

                                                                          Who wrote Akbar Nama?

                                                                          Correct Answer: Abu al Fazal
                                                                          Show explanation
                                                                          Explanation

                                                                          Akbarnama was written by Abul Fazl.

                                                                          It is the official chronicle of Emperor Akbar’s reign (1556–1605).


                                                                          Additional information:

                                                                          • Born: 1551, Agra, India
                                                                          • Died: 1602, Patna, India
                                                                          • Position: Court historian and vizier of Akbar

                                                                          Famous Work: Akbarnama (Three volumes)

                                                                          1. Volume 1: Akbar’s ancestry and early life
                                                                          2. Volume 2: Administrative and political history
                                                                          3. Volume 3: Fatawa-e-Alamgiri / Akbar’s achievements

                                                                          Last verified on 18-01-2026

                                                                          268

                                                                          Hamza Shehbaz is the ______ Chief Minister of Punjab.

                                                                          Correct Answer: 19th
                                                                          Show explanation
                                                                          Explanation
                                                                          • Full Name: Hamza Shahbaz
                                                                          • Order: 19th (17th) Chief Minister of Punjab
                                                                          • Position: Chief Minister of Punjab
                                                                          • Tenure: 30 April 2022 – 26 July 2022 (≈ 3 months)
                                                                          • Administered By: National Assembly Speaker / Legal authority (as per court orders)
                                                                          • Political Party: Pakistan Muslim League (Nawaz)

                                                                          Last verified on 18-01-2026

                                                                          269

                                                                          How many soldiers have been awarded Nishan-e-Haider?

                                                                          Correct Answer: 11
                                                                          Show explanation
                                                                          Explanation

                                                                          Only 11 heroes have received Nishan-e-Haider in Pakistan’s history.

                                                                          Nishan-e-Haider means “Emblem of the Lion”.

                                                                          It is Pakistan’s highest military award for bravery.


                                                                          Additional information:

                                                                          1. Muhammad Sarwar – Punjab Regiment, Captain, 1947 War, 27 July 1948
                                                                          2. Saif Ali Janjua – 5/18 Azad Kashmir Regiment, Naik, 1947 War, 26 October 1948
                                                                          3. Tufail Mohammad – 13 Punjab Regiment, Major, 1958 Skirmishes, 7 August 1958
                                                                          4. Raja Aziz Bhatti – 17 Punjab Regiment, Major, 1965 War, 10 September 1965
                                                                          5. Rashid Minhas – No. 2 Fighter Conversion Unit, Pilot Officer, 1971 War, 20 August 1971
                                                                          6. Muhammad Akram – 4 Frontier Force Regiment, Major, 1971 War, 5 December 1971
                                                                          7. Shabbir Sharif – 6 Frontier Force Regiment, Major, 1971 War, 6 December 1971
                                                                          8. Muhammad Hussain – 20 Lancers, Sowar, 1971 War, 10 December 1971
                                                                          9. Muhammad Mahfuz Janjua – 15 Punjab Regiment, Lance Naik, 1971 War, 17 December 1971
                                                                          10. Karnal Sher Khan – 12 Northern Light Infantry, Captain, Kargil War, 5 July 1999
                                                                          11. Lalak Jan – 12 Northern Light Infantry, Havildar, Kargil War, 7 July 1999

                                                                          Last verified on 18-01-2026

                                                                          • Birth Year: 1207 AD
                                                                          • Death: 17 December 1273 AD (Konya, present-day Turkey)

                                                                            275

                                                                            Famous Urdu poet Mustafa Zaidi was by profession a ?

                                                                            Correct Answer: Civil Servant
                                                                            Show explanation
                                                                            Explanation

                                                                            Famous Urdu poet Mustafa Zaidi was, by profession, a Pakistani civil servant (CSP officer), 


                                                                            He was born in October 10, 1930.


                                                                            He died in October 12, 1970, in Karachi.


                                                                            Pen name: Tegh Allahbadi


                                                                            Notable awards: Tamgha-e-Quaid-e-Azam

                                                                            276

                                                                            According to Article 257 of the Constitution, when the people of Jammu and Kashmir accede to Pakistan then?

                                                                            Correct Answer: People of Kashmir shall determine the relationship between the state and Pakistan
                                                                            Show explanation
                                                                            Explanation

                                                                            Article 257 of the Constitution of Pakistan deals with Kashmir.


                                                                            It outlines the relationship between Pakistan and the State of Jammu and Kashmir


                                                                            Kashmir was established on 24 Oct be 1947


                                                                            District: 10

                                                                            277

                                                                            Taj Mahal is located on the Bank of the river?

                                                                            Correct Answer: Yamuna
                                                                            Show explanation
                                                                            Explanation

                                                                            The Taj Mahal is located in Agra, Uttar Pradesh, India


                                                                            On the west side of the Yamuna River.


                                                                            ADDITIONAL INFORMATION REALTED TAJ MEHAL

                                                                            The Taj Mahal, "Crown of Palaces", also "the Taj"


                                                                            It is a white marble mausoleum.


                                                                            It is located in Agra, Uttar Pradesh, India


                                                                            Construction started

                                                                            1632

                                                                            Construction completed

                                                                            1647

                                                                            Built by

                                                                            Mughal Emperor Shah Jehan

                                                                            Built-in the memory of

                                                                            the memory of his third wife, Mumtaz Mahal.

                                                                            Height

                                                                            73 M

                                                                            Architecture

                                                                            Ustad Ahmed Lahori

                                                                            278

                                                                            Deosai national part is remarkable as it is one of the highest plateaus in the world with an average elevation of 4,114 meters. In which region of Pakistan is it located?

                                                                            Correct Answer: Gilgit-Baltistan
                                                                            Show explanation
                                                                            Explanation

                                                                            Deosai National Park is located in the Western Himalayas in Gilgit-Baltistan


                                                                            It has an average elevation of 4,114 metres (13,497 ft) above sea level,


                                                                            Situated in the bordering Skardu, Gultari, Kharmang, and Astore districts.

                                                                            279

                                                                            LAL Suhanra National park is located near ?

                                                                            Correct Answer: Bahawalpur
                                                                            Show explanation
                                                                            Explanation

                                                                            Lal Suhanra National Park is located near Bahawalpur


                                                                            Known for its diverse desert, forest, and wetland ecosystems. 


                                                                            It is one of Pakistan's largest national parks


                                                                            It is a UNESCO-declared Biosphere Reserve


                                                                            Established 1972

                                                                            280

                                                                            Khudadad Khan was the first South Asian soldier in the British Army to receive the highest military award for gallantry, the Victoria Cross, during the first world war. In which country was he fighting where he performed the act of bravery which earned him the Victoria Cross.

                                                                            Correct Answer: Belgium
                                                                            Show explanation
                                                                            Explanation

                                                                            Khudadad Khan was fighting in Belgium, specifically at Hollebeke, on October 31, 1914


                                                                            He was an Indian recipient of the Victoria Cross


                                                                            Born on 20 October 1888 in Chakwal District, Pakistan


                                                                            Died: 8 March 1971 (aged 82) Mandi Bahauddin

                                                                            281

                                                                            Recently a leading politician, Salahuddin Quader Chaudhry, was handed in Bangladesh. His father, Fazlul Rehman Chaudhry, had served on an important position in Pakistan prior to separation of Bangladesh. Please name the position

                                                                            Correct Answer: Speaker of National Assembly
                                                                            Show explanation
                                                                            Explanation

                                                                            Fazlul Rehman Chaudhry was a prominent Pakistani politician before 1971.


                                                                            He served as the 5th Speaker of the National Assembly of Pakistan from November 29, 1963, to June 12, 1965.


                                                                            He belonged to the Muslim League under Ayub Khan. 

                                                                            282

                                                                            Kamran’s Baradari in Lahore is one of the oldest Mughal monuments. Who exactly was Kamran?

                                                                            Correct Answer: Son of Babur/Stepbrother of Humayun
                                                                            Show explanation
                                                                            Explanation

                                                                            Kamran Mirza was the second son of Babur and a brother to Humayun


                                                                            He was a prince, governor, and poet


                                                                            He built Kamran's Baradari in Lahore around 1540 as a summer pavilion

                                                                            283

                                                                            The youngest son of maharaja Ranjit Singh was elevated as maharaja at a tender age of about 5 years. What was his name?

                                                                            Correct Answer: Duleep Singh
                                                                            Show explanation
                                                                            Explanation

                                                                            The youngest son of Maharaja Ranjit Singh, Maharaja Duleep Singh,


                                                                            He was elevated as Maharaja of the Sikh Empire at the age of five 


                                                                            Maharaja of Punjab, Kashmir, and Jammu from 15 September 1843 to 29 March 1849


                                                                             Nicknamed the "Black Prince of Perthshire"


                                                                            He was the last Maharaja of the Sikh Empire.


                                                                            Born: 6 September 1838, Lahore, Sikh Empire


                                                                            Died: 22 October 1893 (aged 55), Paris, France

                                                                            284

                                                                            Which American Ambassador died with Presidnet Zia Ul Haque in plance crash?

                                                                            Correct Answer: Arnold Raphael
                                                                            Show explanation
                                                                            Explanation

                                                                            American Ambassador Arnold Raphael with President Zia Ul Haque in a plane crash


                                                                            When: August 17, 1988.


                                                                            Where: Near Bahawalpur, Pakistan.


                                                                            Raphel was the 18th U.S. Ambassador to Pakistan from June 24, 1987 – August 17, 1988


                                                                            Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq was served as the sixth president of Pakistan from 1978 until 1988.



                                                                            285

                                                                            Which one of the following districts of Punjab has the lowest population density?

                                                                            Correct Answer: Bahawalpur
                                                                            Show explanation
                                                                            Explanation

                                                                             The Bahawalpur district has the lowest population density 


                                                                            Bahawalpur: 172.3-326 per sq. km (2023) 


                                                                            Rajanpur: ~188.67 persons/km²


                                                                            Layyah: ~334.3 persons/km²


                                                                            D.G. Khan: ~615.7 persons/km² 


                                                                            286

                                                                            G.T. Road is one of the main highways of Pakistan. What does G.T. stand of?

                                                                            Correct Answer: Grand Trunk
                                                                            Show explanation
                                                                            Explanation

                                                                            G.T. Road stands for the Grand Trunk Road,


                                                                            G.T Road was constructed by Sher Shah Suri


                                                                            It is formerly known as Uttarapath, Sarak-e-Azam, Badshahi Sarak, and Sarak-e-Sher Shah


                                                                            It is one of Asia's oldest and longest major roads.


                                                                            It runs roughly, 3,710 km (2,305 mi) from


                                                                            Major junctions

                                                                            • East end: Teknaf, Bangladesh
                                                                            • West end: Kabul, Afghanistan
                                                                            287

                                                                            The Pakistani drama Waris was written by?

                                                                            Correct Answer: Amjad Islam Amjad
                                                                            Show explanation
                                                                            Explanation

                                                                            Pakistani drama Waris, which aired on PTV in 1979


                                                                            He was written by the renowned poet and playwright Amjad Islam Amjad


                                                                            No. of episodes 13


                                                                            Distributor PTV


                                                                            Language: Urdu


                                                                            Country of origin: Pakistan


                                                                            Abid Ali, as Dilawar has performed in one of the lead roles of Waris


                                                                            The first episode was aired from PTV-Lahore on Saturday, 29 December 1979 and the last on 22 March 1980 

                                                                            288

                                                                            What was the rank of former Foreign Minister Gohar Ayub Khan when he left the Army?

                                                                            Correct Answer: Captain
                                                                            Show explanation
                                                                            Explanation

                                                                            Gohar Ayub Khan left the Pakistan Army with the rank of Captain in 1962. 


                                                                            He served in the army from 1957 to 1962.

                                                                            He was the son of Field Marshal Ayub Khan 

                                                                            Leader of the Pakistan Muslim League (N) (2012–2018)

                                                                            14th Speaker of the National Assembly of Pakistan (4 November 1990 – 17 October 1993)

                                                                            Born: 8 January 1937, Rehana (now Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan)

                                                                            Died: 17 November 2023 (aged 86), Islamabad
                                                                            289

                                                                            Abdul Kareem known as Little karim was ______?

                                                                            Correct Answer: Mountaineer
                                                                            Show explanation
                                                                            Explanation
                                                                            • Abdul Kareem (Little Karim) was a mountaineer from Pakistan.
                                                                            • Famous for climbing K2, the second-highest mountain in the world.
                                                                            • Known as “Little Karim” due to his small stature but great climbing skills.

                                                                            Additional information:

                                                                            • Date of Birth: 1953/54
                                                                            • Date of Death: April 4, 2022

                                                                            Last verified on 17-02-2026

                                                                            290

                                                                            Which of the following is responsible for turning Taj Mahal yellow?

                                                                            Correct Answer: Sulphur dioxide
                                                                            Show explanation
                                                                            Explanation

                                                                            The Taj Mahal in Agra, India.

                                                                            It is turning yellow due to air pollution.

                                                                            Main culprit: Sulphur dioxide (SO₂) from industrial emissions and vehicle smoke.


                                                                            Additional information:

                                                                            Taj Mahal made of white Makrana marble.

                                                                            It was built in 1632–1647 by Emperor Shah Jahan.

                                                                            291

                                                                            Chenab meets Jhelum at which place?

                                                                            Correct Answer: Trimmu
                                                                            Show explanation
                                                                            Explanation

                                                                            The Chenab River meets the Jhelum River at Trimmu (Punjab).

                                                                            Chenab originates from the Chandra and Bhaga rivers in the Himalayas, India.

                                                                            Flows through Jammu region and enters Punjab plains in Pakistan.

                                                                            Total length: 1,200 km (750 mi) approx.

                                                                            Historically called Asikni due to dark-colored waters.


                                                                            Additional information:

                                                                            • The area between Jhelum and Chenab is called Chaj Doab.
                                                                            • Rachna Doab: Between Ravi and Chenab
                                                                            • Bari Doab: Between Beas and Ravi

                                                                            Last verified on 17-01-2026

                                                                            292

                                                                            The first gas field Pakistan discovered in 1952 at ______?

                                                                            Correct Answer: Sui
                                                                            Show explanation
                                                                            Explanation
                                                                            • The Sui Gas Field, located near Sui in Balochistan Province, Pakistan.
                                                                              • It was discovered by Pakistan Petroleum Limited in 1952.
                                                                              • Production of Sui gas began in 1955.
                                                                              • It remains the largest natural gas field in Pakistan.
                                                                              293

                                                                              Which of the following institution was established in 1864? MAO college Lahore, Islamia college Lahore, Government college Lahore, None of these

                                                                              Correct Answer: Government College Lahore
                                                                              Show explanation
                                                                              Explanation
                                                                              • Year of Establishment: 1864
                                                                              • Original Name: Government College Lahore
                                                                              • Location: Lahore, Punjab, Pakistan
                                                                              • Famous Alumni: Allama Iqbal, Abdus Salam, Faiz Ahmed Faiz

                                                                              Last verified on 17-01-2026

                                                                              294

                                                                              When Treaty of Amritsar was signed?

                                                                              Correct Answer: 1846
                                                                              Show explanation
                                                                              Explanation
                                                                              • Year Signed: 1846
                                                                              • Parties Involved: East India Company & Raja Gulab Singh
                                                                              • After the: First Anglo-Sikh War (1845–1846)
                                                                              • Payment: Gulab Singh paid 7.5 million Nanakshahee rupees to the British for Kashmir

                                                                              Last verified on 17-01-2026

                                                                              295

                                                                              What is the name of the Prime Minister of India at the time of the 1965 war?

                                                                              Correct Answer: Lal Bahadur Shastri
                                                                              Show explanation
                                                                              Explanation

                                                                            • Indian PM (1965 War): Lal Bahadur Shastri

                                                                            • Pakistani President (1965 War): Ayub Khan

                                                                            • War duration: August – September 1965

                                                                            • Peace agreement: Tashkent Agreement, 10 January 1966

                                                                              • Last verified on 17-01-2026
                                                                              296

                                                                              How much percentage of Kashmir is under the control of India?

                                                                              Correct Answer: 55%
                                                                              Show explanation
                                                                              Explanation
                                                                              • India: 55% (Jammu, Kashmir Valley, Ladakh)
                                                                              • China: 15% (Aksai Chin, Shaksgam Valley)
                                                                              • Pakistan: 30% (Azad Kashmir & Gilgit-Baltistan)
                                                                              • Dispute start: 1947 (Partition of British India)
                                                                              • Line of Actual Control (LAC): Separates Indian and Chinese controlled areas

                                                                              Last verified on 17-01-2026

                                                                              297

                                                                              How much percentage of Kashmir is under the control of China?

                                                                              Correct Answer: 15%
                                                                              Show explanation
                                                                              Explanation
                                                                              • China: 15% (Aksai Chin, Shaksgam Valley)
                                                                              • India: 55% (Jammu, Kashmir Valley, Ladakh)
                                                                              • Pakistan: 30% (Azad Kashmir & Gilgit-Baltistan)
                                                                              • Dispute start: 1947 (Partition of British India)
                                                                              • Line of Actual Control (LAC): Separates Indian and Chinese controlled areas

                                                                              Last verified on 17-01-2026

                                                                              298

                                                                              When Allama Shibli Naumani was died?

                                                                              Correct Answer: November 18, 1914
                                                                              Show explanation
                                                                              Explanation
                                                                              • Died: 18 November 1914
                                                                              • Age at death: 57 years
                                                                              • Burial place: Azamgarh, India

                                                                              Additional information:

                                                                              • Born: 3 June 1857
                                                                              • Associated institution: Nadwatul Ulama

                                                                              Last verified on 17-01-2026

                                                                              299

                                                                              The national flag of Pakistan was adopted on which date?

                                                                              Correct Answer: 11 August 1947
                                                                              Show explanation
                                                                              Explanation
                                                                              • Adoption Date: 11 August 1947 (officially adopted)
                                                                              • Designer: Amir Uddin Kidwai Additional information: Green field – represents Muslims of Pakistan White stripe – represents minorities and religious minorities’ rights Crescent and star – symbol of progress and light Proportion of flag: 2:3 (height:width)

                                                                              Last verified on 17-01-2026

                                                                              300

                                                                              Which of the following state was acceded to Pakistan on 15th August 1947?

                                                                              Correct Answer: Junagarh
                                                                              Show explanation
                                                                              Explanation
                                                                              • Announcement of accession: 15 August 1947
                                                                              • Pakistan’s legal acceptance: 15 September 1947
                                                                              • Ruler: Nawab Muhammad Mahabat Khan III
                                                                              • Region: Kathiawar Peninsula (present-day Gujarat, India)
                                                                              • Link cited: Veraval Port → Karachi (sea route)

                                                                              Additional information:

                                                                              • Plebiscite conducted by India: February 1948
                                                                              • Status after plebiscite: Junagadh annexed by India

                                                                              Last verified on 17-01-2026

                                                                              301

                                                                              How many politicians remain Governor-General of Pakistan?

                                                                              Correct Answer: 4
                                                                              Show explanation
                                                                              Explanation

                                                                              There are 4 politicians remain Governor-General of Pakistan.


                                                                              Additional information:
                                                                              1. Muhammad Ali Jinnah (1947–1948) – Founder of Pakistan, first Governor-General.
                                                                              2. Sir Khawaja Nazimuddin (1948–1951) – Second Governor-General.
                                                                              3. Sir Ghulam Muhammad (1951–1955) – Third Governor-General.
                                                                              4. Iskander Mirza (1955–1956) – Fourth and last Governor-General before Pakistan became a republic.
                                                                              Last verified on 17-01-2026
                                                                              302

                                                                              Mehr-e-Do Neem wrote by ______?

                                                                              Correct Answer: Iftikhar Arif
                                                                              Show explanation
                                                                              Explanation

                                                                              “Mehr-e-Do Neem” was written by Iftikhar Arif.

                                                                              First published in: 1983


                                                                              Additional information:

                                                                              • Born: 12 February 1944, Lucknow, India
                                                                              • Famous poetry collections: “Mehr-e-Do Neem,” “Raqs-e-Khama”
                                                                              • Served as Chairman of Pakistan Academy of Letters
                                                                              • Awards: Pride of Performance (1999), Sitara-i-Imtiaz

                                                                              Last verified on 17-01-2026

                                                                              303

                                                                              Shahr-e-Sukhan Aarasta Hai, wrote by _____?

                                                                              Correct Answer: Ahmad Faraz
                                                                              Show explanation
                                                                              Explanation

                                                                              "Shahr-e-Sukhan Aarasta Hai” was written by Ahmad Faraz.


                                                                              Additional information:

                                                                              • Born: 12 January 1931, Kohat, Pakistan
                                                                              • Died: 25 August 2008, Islamabad
                                                                              • Famous poetry collections: “Tanha Tanha,” “Sab Awazain Meri Hain”
                                                                              • Awards: Pride of Performance (1990), Sitara-i-Imtiaz

                                                                              Last verified on 17-01-2026

                                                                              304

                                                                              Ahmad nadeem Qasmi was not a ______? Columnist, Poet, Novelist, Short story Author

                                                                              Correct Answer: Novelist
                                                                              Show explanation
                                                                              Explanation
                                                                              • Ahmad Nadeem Qasmi was not known as a novelist.
                                                                              • He was a renowned Pakistani writer, poet, and journalist.

                                                                              Additional information:

                                                                              • Born: 1 November 1916, Pasrur, Sialkot
                                                                              • Died: 10 July 2006, Lahore
                                                                              • Famous poetry collection: “Shab-e-Rafta”
                                                                              • Famous short story collection: “Mera Gaon”
                                                                              • Edited literary magazine: “Funoon”

                                                                              Last verified on 17-01-2026

                                                                              305

                                                                              Pakistan's Foreign Policy Dilemma written by _____?

                                                                              Correct Answer: Shamshad Ahmed
                                                                              Show explanation

                                                                              • Book Title: Pakistan's Foreign Policy Dilemma
                                                                              • Author: Shamshad Ahmed
                                                                              • Field: Foreign Policy / International Relations
                                                                              • Focus Areas: Security, diplomacy, global relations

                                                                              Additional information:

                                                                              • He served as Foreign Secretary of Pakistan (1997 to 2000).
                                                                              • He served as the Permanent Representative of Pakistan to the United Nations

                                                                              Last verified on 17-01-2026

                                                                              306

                                                                              Which of the following species is endemic to Pakistan? Snow Leopards , Indus River Dolphins ,Gypsy Vultures

                                                                              Correct Answer: Indus River Dolphins
                                                                              Show explanation
                                                                              Explanation

                                                                              The Indus River Dolphin (Platanista minor) is largely endemic to the Indus River


                                                                              The Indus river dolphin was described in 1853 by Richard Owen under the name Platanista gangetica,

                                                                               

                                                                              Also known as "bhulan" in Pakistan.

                                                                              It was the first discovered side-swimming cetacean

                                                                              307

                                                                              Ustad Allah Baksh was a famous for in Pakistan?

                                                                              Correct Answer: Painter
                                                                              Show explanation
                                                                              Explanation

                                                                              Ustad Allah Baksh was famous in Pakistan as a pioneering painter and calligrapher


                                                                              Awards : Pride of Performance (1963) and Tamgha-e-Imtiaz (1956)


                                                                              He was born around 1895 in Wazirabad


                                                                              Died: 18 October 1978 (aged 82–83) Lahore

                                                                              308

                                                                              Advisor to the Prime Minister on Foreign Affairs Sartaj Aziz holds a Master’s degree in Development Economics from which university?

                                                                              Correct Answer: Harvard
                                                                              Show explanation
                                                                              Explanation

                                                                               Sartaj Aziz, the former Advisor to the Prime Minister on Foreign Affairs From  7 June 2013 – 28 July 2017


                                                                              Master's degree in Development Economics from Harvard University, USA, in 1963


                                                                              6th National Security Advisor from 7 June 2013 – 23 October 2015


                                                                              Born in 1929 in Nowshera, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa.


                                                                              Died: 2 January 2024 (aged 94) Islamabad

                                                                              309

                                                                              Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif proposed a new peace initiative with India including ceasefire on LOC, non-use of force, demilitarization of Kashmir and?

                                                                              Correct Answer: Withdrawal of forces from Siachen
                                                                              Show explanation
                                                                              Explanation

                                                                              The United Nations General Assembly on September 30, 2015,


                                                                              Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif proposed a new four-point peace initiative with India, which included: 

                                                                              1. Formalizing and respecting the 2003 ceasefire on the Line of Control (LoC).
                                                                              2. Reaffirming the non-use of force.
                                                                              3. Demilitarization of Kashmir.
                                                                              4. Unconditional withdrawal of forces from Siachen.

                                                                              • Year of Constitution Approval: 1973, April 10
                                                                              • Golden Jubilee Declaration: 2023, April 10
                                                                              • Significance: Commemorates the country’s supreme law and democratic framework

                                                                                    321

                                                                                    Who was the second Governor-General of Pakistan?

                                                                                    Correct Answer: Sir Khawaja Nazimuddin
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation

                                                                                    Second Governor-General: Sir Khawaja Nazimuddin.

                                                                                    From 14 September 1948 to 16 October 1951.

                                                                                    Party:

                                                                                    CML (1962–1964)

                                                                                    ML (1947–1958)

                                                                                    AIML (1922–1947)


                                                                                    Additional information:
                                                                                    1. Muhammad Ali Jinnah (1947–1948) – Founder of Pakistan, first Governor-General.
                                                                                    2. Sir Khawaja Nazimuddin (1948–1951) – Second Governor-General.
                                                                                    3. Sir Ghulam Muhammad (1951–1955) – Third Governor-General.
                                                                                    4. Iskander Mirza (1955–1956) – Fourth and last Governor-General before Pakistan became a republic.
                                                                                    Last updated by NA on 23-April-2026

                                                                                    322

                                                                                    What is the limit of Pakistan's Continental Shelf?

                                                                                    Correct Answer: 350 NM
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation
                                                                                    • Pakistan’s continental shelf officially extends up to 350 nautical miles from its baseline.
                                                                                    • A continental shelf is the extended perimeter of a country’s land under the sea.

                                                                                    Additional information:

                                                                                    • Pakistan submitted its extended shelf claim to the UN in 2015.
                                                                                    • The EEZ (Exclusive Economic Zone) limit is 200 NM, but the continental shelf can extend further.

                                                                                    Last verified on 15-01-2026

                                                                                    323

                                                                                    Wakhan Corridor is located to the _____ of Pakistan.

                                                                                    Correct Answer: North
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation
                                                                                    • Wakhan Corridor is located to the north of Pakistan.
                                                                                    • It is a narrow strip of land in Afghanistan that separates Pakistan from Tajikistan.
                                                                                    • It is strategically important and connects Central Asia with South Asia.

                                                                                    Last verified on 16-01-2026

                                                                                    324

                                                                                    Operation Zarb-e-Azb underwent in the former FATA. Azb was the name of ______?

                                                                                    Correct Answer: Sword of the Holy Prophet (PBUH)
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation
                                                                                    • ‘Azb’ refers to one of the seven swords of the Holy Prophet (PBUH) used in Ghazwaz of Uhud and Badr.
                                                                                    • Operation Zarb-e-Azb (15 June 2014 – 22 Feb 2017) was launched in North Waziristan, former FATA, Pakistan.
                                                                                    • Operation was led by Army Chief General Raheel Sharif to eliminate terrorist groups.

                                                                                    Additional information:
                                                                                    • Operation Radd-ul-Fasaad followed on 22 Feb 2017, led by General Qamar Javid Bajwa.
                                                                                    Last verified on 16-01-2026
                                                                                    325

                                                                                    When was the Awaam Raj Party established?

                                                                                    Correct Answer: 15 January 2026
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation
                                                                                    • Launch Date: 15 January 2026, at a press conference in Lahore Press Club
                                                                                    • Founder / Leader: Iqrar ul Hassan, renowned journalist and Sar-e-Aam host
                                                                                    • Party Name: Pakistan Awaam Raj Tehreek (also called Awam Raj Party)
                                                                                    • Symbol / Identity: Introduced with a red flag representing the party
                                                                                    • Core Vision: A people-powered platform to empower ordinary citizens—workers, farmers, and youth—over traditional political and military elites

                                                                                    Last verified on 15-01-2026

                                                                                    326

                                                                                    The poetry first written by Allama Iqbal in Persian and then translated into Urdu?

                                                                                    Correct Answer: Bang-e-Dara
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation

                                                                                    Bang-e-Dara (The Call of the Caravan): His first collection of Urdu poetry,


                                                                                    Iqbal's Urdu poetry collection is available in four books.

                                                                                    1. Bang-i-Dara 1924
                                                                                    2. Bal-e-Jibril 1935
                                                                                    3. Zarb-i-Kalim 1936
                                                                                    4. Armaghan-e-Hijaz 1938

                                                                                    327

                                                                                    The largest earth filled dam of the world is _____?

                                                                                    Correct Answer: Tarbela dam
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation

                                                                                    The Tarbela Dam in Pakistan is the largest earth-filled dam in the world.

                                                                                    It is built on the Indus River.


                                                                                    Additional information:

                                                                                    • Location: Haripur District, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan
                                                                                    • Completed: 1976
                                                                                    • Length: 2,743 meters (longest earth-filled dam)
                                                                                    • Height: 143 meters
                                                                                    • Purpose: Irrigation, hydroelectric power (3,478 MW), flood control

                                                                                    Last verified on 15-01-2026

                                                                                    328

                                                                                    Captain of Pakistan cricket team that defeated England at Oval in 1954 was?

                                                                                    Correct Answer: Abdul Hafeez Kardar
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation

                                                                                    The captain of the Pakistan cricket team that defeated England at The Oval in 1954 was A. H. Kardar 


                                                                                    Known as "The Skipper"


                                                                                    He led the Pakistan cricket team in its first 23 Test matches


                                                                                    Spanning from 1952 to 1958


                                                                                    Born: 17 January 1925, Lahore


                                                                                    Died: 21 April 1996 (aged 71), Islamabad

                                                                                    329

                                                                                    When Ayub Khan took over power in 1958, the Prime Minister was?

                                                                                    Correct Answer: Malik Feroze Khan Noon
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation

                                                                                    Ayub Khan took power in October 1958.


                                                                                    The Prime Minister of Pakistan was indeed Malik Feroz Khan Noon


                                                                                    Malik Feroz Khan Noon served as Prime Minister from December 1957 until October 7, 1958.


                                                                                    Ayub Khan then became the head of administration, replacing Noon in October 1958. 

                                                                                    330

                                                                                    Who was not member of Punjab Board of Administration?

                                                                                    Correct Answer: Robert Montgomery
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation

                                                                                    Robert Montgomery was not an initial member of the Punjab Board of Administration.


                                                                                    The original three members were

                                                                                    • Henry Lawrence (President)
                                                                                    • John Lawrence (Junior member)
                                                                                    • Charles Mansel (Senior member, administered justice)

                                                                                    ADDITIONAL INFORMATION RELATED BORAD OF ADMINISTRATION


                                                                                    • The Board of Administration was abolished in 1853 and replaced by the office of the Chief Commissioner
                                                                                    • Inaugural holder: Henry Lawrence
                                                                                    • Formation: 1 April 1849
                                                                                    • Final holder: Evan Meredith Jenkins
                                                                                    • Abolished: 15 August 1947
                                                                                    331

                                                                                    Office of District Magistrate was abolished through?

                                                                                    Correct Answer: Local Government Ordinance 2001
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation

                                                                                    The Pakistan Administrative Service was abolished through the Devolution of Power Plan (2001)


                                                                                    Previously known as the District Management Group (DMG before 1 June 2012


                                                                                    Implemented under President Musharraf via the Punjab Local Government Ordinance (PLGO 2001) and Police Order (2002)

                                                                                    Correct Answer: M. Raziuddin
                                                                                    Show explanation

                                                                                    M. Raziuddin collaborated with Einstein on scientific and mathematical research.


                                                                                    Additional information:

                                                                                    • Profession: Physicist / Mathematician
                                                                                    • Contribution: Physics research, mentoring scientists in Pakistan

                                                                                    • Contributions: Strengthened Pakistan–US diplomatic and economic relations

                                                                                    Last verified on 15-01-2026

                                                                                    336

                                                                                    The Quran and Sunnah are declared the Supreme law of Pakistan under the which Amendment?

                                                                                    Correct Answer: 9th
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation

                                                                                    The 9th Constitutional Amendment (1985) declared that the Quran and Sunnah are the supreme law of Pakistan.


                                                                                    Additional information:

                                                                                    • President: Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq
                                                                                    • Purpose: Islamization of laws
                                                                                    • Scope: All laws in Pakistan must conform to Quran and Sunnah

                                                                                    Last verified on 15-01-2026

                                                                                    337

                                                                                    Which country assisted Pakistan in building Karachi Nuclear Power Plant?

                                                                                    Correct Answer: Canada
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation

                                                                                    Pakistan’s first nuclear power plant, KANUPP, was built in Karachi with Canada’s assistance.

                                                                                    Inaugurated on 28 November 1972 with a capacity of 137 MW.


                                                                                    Additional information:

                                                                                    Later, K2 and K3 nuclear plants were built with China’s help.

                                                                                    K2 started operations on 21 May 2021 with 1100 MW capacity.

                                                                                    K3 is expected to start soon, further boosting Pakistan’s nuclear energy.


                                                                                    Last verified on 15-01-2026

                                                                                    338

                                                                                    _____ had signed Pakistan's boundary agreement with China.

                                                                                    Correct Answer: Zulifqar Ali Bhutto
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation
                                                                                    • Signed by: Chen Yi (China), Zulfikar Ali Bhutto (Pakistan)
                                                                                      • Signed on: 2 March 1963
                                                                                      • Length of border: 599 km
                                                                                      • Era of initial demarcation: Ayub Khan’s rule

                                                                                      Last verified on 15-01-2026
                                                                                    339

                                                                                    Who was not a member of the 1st Cabinet of Pakistan?

                                                                                    Correct Answer: Hussain Shaheed Suharwardy
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation

                                                                                    Huseyn Shaheed Suhrawardy was not a member of Pakistan's very first cabinet in 1947


                                                                                    There are the following member including in the first cabinet

                                                                                    1. Liaquat Ali Khan (Prime Minister, Foreign Minister, Defence Minister, and Minister for Commonwealth Relations)
                                                                                    2.  Malik Ghulam Mohammad (Finance Minister)
                                                                                    3. Sardar Abdul Rab Nishtar (Transport and Communication Minister)
                                                                                    4. Raja Ghazanfar Ali Khan (Minister for Food, Agriculture, and Health)
                                                                                    5. Jugindarnath Mandal (Minister for Law and Labour)
                                                                                    6. Fazal-ur-Rehman (Interior Minister and Minister for Information and Education)
                                                                                    7.  Ibrahim Ismail Chundrigarh (Minister for Trade, Industry and Construction)


                                                                                    340

                                                                                    The annual average flow of water in Indus River system is?

                                                                                    Correct Answer: 140 MAF
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation

                                                                                    The annual average flow of water in the Indus River system is approximately 140 MAF (million acre-feet).


                                                                                    ADDITIONAL INFORMATION RELATED TO INDUS RIVER 

                                                                                    • The Indus River starts in Tibet.
                                                                                    • The river Indus originates from the Mansarovar lake.
                                                                                    • The Indus River flows through India, Pakistan,
                                                                                    • The Indus River finally falls into the Arabian Sea near Karachi.
                                                                                    • River Indus starts making a delta near the city of Thatta.
                                                                                    • The old name of the Indus river was: Sindhu
                                                                                    • National River of Pakistan: Indus River
                                                                                    341

                                                                                    Khojak pass connects ________?

                                                                                    Correct Answer: Qila Abdullah with Chaman
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation
