                                                                                    Khojak Pass connects Qila Abdullah with Chaman

                                                                                    The construction started in 1888

                                                                                    The construction  was completed in 1891

                                                                                    It was once the 4th-longest tunnel in the world


                                                                                    Khojak Tunnel

                                                                                    The location 

                                                                                    Balochistan

                                                                                    The length 

                                                                                    3.9 KM

                                                                                     longest Railway Tunnel of

                                                                                    Pakistan

                                                                                    known as

                                                                                    Shela Bagh Tunnel

                                                                                     located near the city of

                                                                                    West of Quetta

                                                                                    Note: The State Bank of Pakistan recognized the marvel of the Khojak Tunnel and a picture of it was printed on the five-rupee note in 1976, which remained in circulation until 2005.


                                                                                    342

                                                                                    Which is the largest Tribal Agency of FATA in terms of area?

                                                                                    Correct Answer: South Waziristan
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation

                                                                                  • South Waziristan is the largest agency.

                                                                                  • It was merged into KP in 2018.

                                                                                  • It is located in the former FATA.

                                                                                  • It borders Afghanistan.

                                                                                  • South Waziristan District is a district in Dera Ismail Khan.

                                                                                    • QUESTION UPDATED 14 Januray 2026

                                                                                    343

                                                                                    Quaid-e-Azam resigned from Imperial Legislative Council due to _____?

                                                                                    Correct Answer: Rowlatt Act
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation
                                                                                    • Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah resigned from the Imperial Legislative Council in 1919.
                                                                                    • His resignation was a protest against the Rowlatt Act.
                                                                                    • He termed the Act as “a black law” because it curtailed civil liberties.

                                                                                    Additional information:

                                                                                    • 1919 – Rowlatt Act passed
                                                                                    • 1919 – Jallianwala Bagh incident
                                                                                    • 1916 – Lucknow Pact
                                                                                    • 1928 – Simon Commission report

                                                                                    Last Updated on 13-01-2026

                                                                                    344

                                                                                    Identify the most benefited sector of Pakistan because of the GPS Plus (Duty Free Access to European Union)?

                                                                                    Correct Answer: Textile and Garments
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation
                                                                                    • GSP Plus (Generalised Scheme of Preferences Plus) gives Pakistan duty-free access to the European Union market.
                                                                                    • Pakistan received GSP Plus status in 2014.
                                                                                    • The textile and garments sector gained the maximum export benefit.

                                                                                    Additional information:

                                                                                    • Over 70% of Pakistan’s exports to the EU are from the textile sector.
                                                                                    • GSP Plus requires compliance with 27 international conventions on human rights, labor laws, environment, and governance.

                                                                                    Last Updated on 13-01-2026

                                                                                    345

                                                                                    What was Dr. Ruth Pfau famously known as?

                                                                                    Correct Answer: Mother of Leprosy Patients
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation

                                                                                    Dr. Ruth Pfau

                                                                                    • Date of Birth: 9 September 1929
                                                                                    • Date of Death: 10 August 2017
                                                                                    • Other Name: Mother Teresa of Pakistan (due to her work for leprosy patients)

                                                                                    Major Awards:

                                                                                    • Hilal-e-Imtiaz (Pakistan)
                                                                                    • Hilal-e-Pakistan
                                                                                    • Nishan-e-Quaid-i-Azam
                                                                                    • Staufer Medal (Germany)
                                                                                    • Dame of the Order of St. Gregory the Great (by Pope)

                                                                                    Last Updated on 13-01-2026

                                                                                    346

                                                                                    Renowned Urdu writer Mushtaq Ahmed Yusufi by profession was _____?

                                                                                    Correct Answer: Banker
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation
                                                                                    • Mushtaq Ahmed Yusufi was a renowned Urdu humorist and writer.
                                                                                    • By profession, he was a banker, not a full-time writer.
                                                                                    • He was the President of Bank of Credit and Commerce International (BCCI).
                                                                                    • He is famous for works like “Aab-e-Gum” and “Zarguzasht”.

                                                                                    Last Updated on 13-01-2026

                                                                                    347

                                                                                    Hibba Khatoon is famous poetess of which language?

                                                                                    Correct Answer: Kashmiri
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation
                                                                                    • Hibba Khatoon was a famous Kashmiri poetess of the 16th century.
                                                                                    • She is known for her lyrical and emotional poetry in the Kashmiri language.
                                                                                    • Her poetry reflects love, separation, and human emotions.

                                                                                    Last Updated on 13-01-2026

                                                                                    348

                                                                                    Dr. Abdul Salam, 1st Nobel Prize recipient of Pakistan got his Ph.D degree from which university?

                                                                                    Correct Answer: University of Cambridge
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation
                                                                                    • Dr. Abdus Salam was the first Nobel Prize recipient of Pakistan.
                                                                                    • He earned his Ph.D. from the University of Cambridge (UK).
                                                                                    • He received the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1979 for his work in electroweak theory.

                                                                                    Last Updated on 13-01-2026

                                                                                    349

                                                                                    The British sold the Kashmir to Gulab Singh Dogra under the treaty of _____?

                                                                                    Correct Answer: Treaty of Ameristar
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation

                                                                                  • The British sold Kashmir to Gulab Singh Dogra under the Treaty of Amritsar.

                                                                                  • The treaty was signed in 1846, after the First Anglo-Sikh War.

                                                                                  • Buyer: Gulab Singh Dogra

                                                                                  • Seller: British East India Company

                                                                                  • Amount Paid: 7.5 million Nanakshahi rupees


                                                                                    Last Updated on 13-01-2026

                                                                                    • 350

                                                                                    Which of the following women participated in all three Round table Conferences?

                                                                                    Correct Answer: Begum Jahan Ara Shahnawaz
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation
                                                                                    • Begum Jahan Ara Shahnawaz was the only Muslim woman who participated in all three Round Table Conferences.
                                                                                    • These conferences were held in London (1930–1932) to discuss constitutional reforms in British India.
                                                                                    • She was a leader of the All India Muslim League.

                                                                                    Last Updated on 13-01-2026

                                                                                    351

                                                                                    On the advice of Prime Minister, Chief Minister, President and Governor are bound to dissolve assemblies within _____?

                                                                                    Correct Answer: 48 hours
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation
                                                                                    • Under the Constitution of Pakistan, the President and Governor must dissolve the assembly on the advice of the Prime Minister or Chief Minister.
                                                                                    • If they do not act, the assembly automatically dissolves after 48 hours.
                                                                                    • This rule is mentioned in Article 58 (1) and Article 112 (1) of the Constitution.

                                                                                    Additional information:

                                                                                    • 6 months – Maximum time to hold general elections
                                                                                    • 342 – Total seats of National Assembly
                                                                                    • 4 – Provincial Assemblies
                                                                                    • 6 years – Senate term

                                                                                    Last Updated on 13-01-2026

                                                                                    352

                                                                                    Which gulf state has denoted 320 MW gas fueled power plant to Pakistan?

                                                                                    Correct Answer: UAE
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation

                                                                                    The United Arab Emirates (UAE) gifted a 320 MW dual-fuel (gas/oil) power plant to Pakistan to help ease power shortages.

                                                                                    The plant was installed in Faisalabad. 


                                                                                    353

                                                                                    If Kerry Lugar Bill passed, how much Pakistan will get from USA?

                                                                                    Correct Answer: 1.5 billion $
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation

                                                                                    If the Kerry-Lugar Bill has received $1.5 billion in non-military aid annually for five years


                                                                                    The 'Enhanced Partnership with Pakistan Act of 2009


                                                                                    It is also known as the Kerry-Lugar-Berman Act


                                                                                    It was passed by the US Congress on 24 September and signed by President Obama on 15 October.


                                                                                    It was proposed by Senators John Kerry (D-Massachusetts) and Richard Lugar (R-Indiana).


                                                                                    354

                                                                                    Pakistan government has decided to celebrate 2009 as the year of?

                                                                                    Correct Answer: Justice
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation

                                                                                    Pakistan's government, specifically the National Judicial (Policy Making) Committee,

                                                                                    Designated 2009 as a year focused on achieving Justice at the Grassroots Level

                                                                                    They aimed at improving access and fairness in the justice system. 

                                                                                    355

                                                                                    When the TV Transmission started in the country?

                                                                                    Correct Answer: November 26 1964
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation

                                                                                    On November 26, 1964, the first official television station commenced transmission broadcasts from Lahore

                                                                                    Inauguration: By President Mohammad Ayub Khan.

                                                                                    Technology: Initial broadcasts were in black and white

                                                                                    356

                                                                                    What is the minimum age of Senator under 1973 Constitution?

                                                                                    Correct Answer: 30 years
                                                                                    Show explanation
                                                                                    Explanation
                                                                                    • Under the 1973 Constitution of Pakistan, the minimum age for a Senator is 30 years.
                                                                                    • The condition is mentioned in Article 62 of the Constitution.

                                                                                      Additional information:
                                                                                      Member National Assembly (MNA): 25 years
                                                                                      Prime Minister: Must be an MNA (minimum 25 years)
                                                                                      President of Pakistan: 45 years

                                                                                      Last Updated on 12-01-2026

                                                                                      357

                                                                                      Who was the only Vice President in the history of Pakistan?

                                                                                      Correct Answer: Nur-ul-Amin
                                                                                      Show explanation
                                                                                      Explanation

                                                                                      • Full Name: Nurul Amin

                                                                                      • Born: 15 July 1893, Shahbazpur, Bengal Presidency, British India

                                                                                      • Died: 2 October 1974 (aged 81), Rawalpindi, Punjab, Pakistan

                                                                                      • Eighth Prime Minister of Pakistan:

                                                                                        • Term: 7 December 1971 – 20 December 1971

                                                                                        • Duration: 13 days (shortest tenure of any Pakistani Prime Minister)

                                                                                      • First and Only Vice President of Pakistan:

                                                                                        • Term: 20 December 1971 – 14 August 1973

                                                                                        Last Updated on 12-01-2026

                                                                                        358

                                                                                        Who was the first muslim governor of the province Punjab was?

                                                                                        Correct Answer: Abdur Rab Nishtar
                                                                                        Show explanation
                                                                                        Explanation
                                                                                        • First Muslim Governor of Punjab: August 1949 – November 1951
                                                                                        • Appointed Governor of Punjab after the dismissal of the Punjab provincial government in 1949.

                                                                                        Additional information:
                                                                                        • Born: 13 June 1899, Peshawar
                                                                                        • Died: 14 February 1958 (aged 58), Karachi

                                                                                        Last Updated on 12-01-2026

                                                                                        359

                                                                                        The Chairman of National Economic Council is _____?

                                                                                        Correct Answer: Prime Minister
                                                                                        Show explanation
                                                                                        Explanation

                                                                                        • The National Economic Council (NEC) of Pakistan is chaired by the Prime Minister.

                                                                                        • It is a constitutional body formed under Article 156 of the Constitution of Pakistan.

                                                                                        • The council is responsible for economic planning and development policies.

                                                                                        NEC 5 Key Members:

                                                                                        • Prime Minister – Chairman

                                                                                        • Chief Ministers of Punjab, Sindh, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Balochistan

                                                                                        • Federal Ministers related to finance and planning


                                                                                          Last Updated on 12-01-2026

                                                                                        360

                                                                                        River Kabul joins Indus at?

                                                                                        Correct Answer: Attock
                                                                                        Show explanation
                                                                                        Explanation
                                                                                        • River Kabul flows from Afghanistan into Khyber Pakhtunkhwa before joining Indus.
                                                                                        • River Kabul passes through Kabul (Afghanistan), Jalalabad, and Peshawar.

                                                                                          Additional information:
                                                                                        • Indus River – Longest river of Pakistan
                                                                                        • Jhelum River – Joins Indus at Trimmu
                                                                                        • Chenab River – Joins Indus at Panjnad
                                                                                        • Ravi River – Tributary of Indus
                                                                                        • Sutlej River – Joins Indus at Panjnad

                                                                                        Last Updated on 12-01-2026

                                                                                        361

                                                                                        The Mausoleum of Khawja Ghulam Farid is located in ____?

                                                                                        Correct Answer: Mithankot (Rajanpur)
                                                                                        Show explanation
                                                                                        Explanation
                                                                                        • Born: 1845, Chachran, Bahawalpur, Punjab, Pakistan
                                                                                          • Died: 24 July 1901 (aged 56), Chachran, Bahawalpur
                                                                                          • Resting Place / Mausoleum: Mithankot, Punjab
                                                                                          • Sufi Order: Member of Chishti Nizami Sufi order

                                                                                          Last Updated on 12-01-2026
                                                                                        362

                                                                                        When karakorum Highway was completed?

                                                                                        Correct Answer: 1979
                                                                                        Show explanation
                                                                                        Explanation
                                                                                        • The Karakoram Highway (KKH), also called the "Eighth Wonder of the World".
                                                                                        • It was completed in 1979.
                                                                                        • Construction started in 1966 and took 13 years to complete.
                                                                                        • It connects Pakistan (Gilgit-Baltistan) with China (Xinjiang) through the Karakoram mountain range.
                                                                                        • The highway is 1,300 km long.

                                                                                        Last Updated on 12-01-2026

                                                                                        363

                                                                                        Shandur Pass connects which of the following?

                                                                                        Correct Answer: Chitral with Gilgit
                                                                                        Show explanation
                                                                                        Explanation
                                                                                        Shandur Pass is a high mountain pass at an altitude of 3,700 meters (12,140 ft).
                                                                                        It connects the Chitral District of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa with Gilgit-Baltistan.
                                                                                        Famous for the annual Shandur Polo Festival, held every July.

                                                                                        Additional Points:
                                                                                        Shandur is known as the “Roof of the World” because of its high altitude.
                                                                                        The pass remains snowbound in winter.
                                                                                        It is usually open from June to September.

                                                                                        Last Updated on 12-01-2026
                                                                                        364

                                                                                        Faiz Ahmed Faiz was arrested in Rawalpindi Conspiracy Case in ______?

                                                                                        Correct Answer: 1951
                                                                                        Show explanation
                                                                                        Explanation

                                                                                        Faiz Ahmed Faiz, a renowned Pakistani poet and intellectual.

                                                                                        He was arrested in 1951 in connection with the Rawalpindi Conspiracy Case.

                                                                                        This political conspiracy was alleged by the government of Liaquat Ali Khan.

                                                                                        The case involved allegations of a coup plot against the government.

                                                                                        365

                                                                                        Ahmad Khan Kharal the famous character of War of Independence belongs to _____?

                                                                                        Correct Answer: Jhamra
                                                                                        Show explanation
                                                                                        Explanation

                                                                                        Ahmad Khan Kharal was a leading freedom fighter of the War of Independence (1857).

                                                                                        He led resistance against British rule in Punjab.

                                                                                        He belonged to Jhamra.

                                                                                        Jhamra is a village of the Faisalabad District in Punjab, Pakistan.


                                                                                        Additional Details / Facts

                                                                                        He played a key role in uprisings around Gogera region.

                                                                                        Martyred while fighting the British in 1857.

                                                                                        366

                                                                                        Mustafa Kamal abolished Khilafat in _____?

                                                                                        Correct Answer: 1924
                                                                                        Show explanation
                                                                                        Explanation

                                                                                        Mustafa Kemal Atatürk abolished the Khilafat (Caliphate) in 1924.

                                                                                        This was part of his modernization and secularization reforms in Turkey.

                                                                                        The abolition marked the end of the Ottoman Caliphate system.


                                                                                        Additional Details / Facts

                                                                                        Capital of Turkey moved from Istanbul to Ankara.

                                                                                        Introduced Western-style legal and educational systems.

                                                                                        Paved the way for the Republic of Turkey.

                                                                                        367

                                                                                        Rakh Ghulaman Life Stock Farm is located in _____?

                                                                                        Correct Answer: Bhakkar
                                                                                        Show explanation
                                                                                        Explanation

                                                                                        Rakh Ghulaman Livestock Farm is located in Bhakkar, Pakistan.

                                                                                        It is a large livestock breeding and research farm.

                                                                                        Focuses on cattle, sheep, and goat farming.


                                                                                        Additional Details / Facts

                                                                                        Supports dairy and meat production in Pakistan.

                                                                                        Contributes to agricultural research and livestock improvement.

                                                                                        Managed under Punjab Livestock & Dairy Development Department.

                                                                                        368

                                                                                        ‘Naxalite’ movement is a pro communism movement started in?

                                                                                        Correct Answer: India
                                                                                        Show explanation
                                                                                        Explanation

                                                                                        The ‘Naxalite’ movement is a pro-communist armed rebellion

                                                                                        Began in 1967 in the Naxalbari village of West Bengal, India.

                                                                                        History 

                                                                                        - In 1967, a faction of the Communist Party of India (Marxist) broke away, known as the "Siliguri Group".  

                                                                                        - The group was led by Charu Majumdar, Kanu Sanyal, and Jangal Santhal.  

                                                                                        - They launched a movement for a protracted people's war in India, inspired by the Chinese Communist Revolution.  

                                                                                        - Charu Majumdar wrote the Historic Eight Documents, which became the ideological foundation of the Naxalite Movement.  

                                                                                        - The movement began in Naxalbari, West Bengal — hence the name “Naxalite”.

                                                                                        369

                                                                                        Who was the founder of the All-India Muslim League?

                                                                                        Correct Answer: Nawab Khwaja Salimullah
                                                                                        Show explanation
                                                                                        Explanation
                                                                                        • The All India Muslim League was founded on 30 December 1906 in Dacca, British India (now Dhaka, Bangladesh).
                                                                                          • It's founder was Nawab Khwaja Salimullah.
                                                                                          • It's first President was Sir Agha Khan.
                                                                                          • The headquarters of the All India Muslim League was in Lucknow.
                                                                                          • The party was dissolved on 14 August 1947.

                                                                                          When the Muslim League ended in 1947, it split into three parties:

                                                                                          1. Muslim League in Pakistan
                                                                                          2. Awami League in Bangladesh
                                                                                          3. Indian Union Muslim League in India
                                                                                          370

                                                                                          Which of the following provinces of Afghanistan share border with Pakistan?

                                                                                          Correct Answer: Ningrahar, Paktia, Ghazni
                                                                                          Show explanation
                                                                                          Explanation

                                                                                          The following provinces of Afghanistan share a border with Pakistan:-

                                                                                          Eastern Border (Khyber Pakhtunkhwa region of Pakistan):

                                                                                          1. Nangarhar: Borders Pakistan's Khyber Pakhtunkhwa; a major crossing point is Torkham. 
                                                                                          2. Kunar: Also borders Pakistan's KP. 
                                                                                          3. Paktia & Khost: Share borders with Pakistan's KP. 
                                                                                          4. Paktika: Borders Pakistan's Balochistan and KP. 

                                                                                          Southern Border (Balochistan region of Pakistan):

                                                                                          1. Zabul: Borders Pakistan's Balochistan. 
                                                                                          2. Kandahar: Borders Pakistan's Balochistan, with the Spin Boldak crossing. 
                                                                                          3. Helmand: Borders Pakistan's Balochistan. 
                                                                                          4. Nimruz: Also borders Pakistan's Balochistan. 
                                                                                          371

                                                                                          Working boundary is the border between Pakistan and _______?

                                                                                          Correct Answer: Indian-occupied Kashmir
                                                                                          Show explanation
                                                                                          Explanation

                                                                                          It is the line between Pakistan along Sialkot and Indian-held Kashmir.

                                                                                          It is called a working boundary because:

                                                                                          • on one side is internationally recognized land (Sialkot)
                                                                                          • on the other is a disputed territory. 



                                                                                          372

                                                                                          Which is the largest/biggest barrage of Pakistan?

                                                                                          Correct Answer: Sukkur
                                                                                          Show explanation
                                                                                          Explanation

                                                                                          The Sukkur Barrage is the largest in Pakistan.

                                                                                          It Is the pride of Pakistan's Irrigation system and in the whole world.


                                                                                          Additional information:

                                                                                          Sukkur Barrage is a barrage on the River Indus 

                                                                                          The barrage was built during the British Raj

                                                                                          Build from 1923 to 1932 

                                                                                          Its old name was named Lloyd Barrage.

                                                                                          It is located in Sindh Province

                                                                                          373

                                                                                          Before the partition of India in 1947 how many Princely states existed?

                                                                                          Correct Answer: 562
                                                                                          Show explanation
                                                                                          Explanation
                                                                                          The largest Princely state by area at the time of partition was Kashmir.

                                                                                          Out of 562 princely states during 1947, 13 princely states decided to join Pakistan.

                                                                                          ****

                                                                                          These 13 princely states are:

                                                                                              Bahawalpur: On 3 October 1947, the Nawab of Bahawalpur, Sadeq Mohammad Khan V, acceded his state to Pakistan, becoming the first ruler to do so successfully.

                                                                                              Khairpur: The state of Khairpur acceded to Pakistan on 3 October 1947.

                                                                                              Chitral: The Mehtar of Chitral, Muzaffar-ul-Mulk, stated his intention to accede to Pakistan on 15 August 1947. On 28 July 1969, President Yahya Khan announced the full integration

                                                                                                RK15.06.2023

                                                                                                374

                                                                                                The height of K-2 the second highest mountain is _______ feet.

                                                                                                Correct Answer: 28,251 feet
                                                                                                Show explanation
                                                                                                Explanation

                                                                                                ·         K2 also called Mount Godwin Austen, called locally Dapsang or Chogori,

                                                                                                ·         The world's second-highest peak

                                                                                                ·         K2 height in feet 28,251

                                                                                                ·         K2 height in meters 8,611 meters

                                                                                                ·         K2 second only to Mount Everest.

                                                                                                375

                                                                                                The members of constituent assembly was increased from 69 to _____?

                                                                                                Correct Answer: 79
                                                                                                Show explanation
                                                                                                Explanation
                                                                                                The membership of the Constituent Assembly was increased from 69 to 79 in 1948.
                                                                                                This allowed additional representatives from various regions of Pakistan.
                                                                                                The assembly worked under the Government of India Act 1935 initially.
                                                                                                It played a key role in framing Pakistan’s first constitution in 1956.
                                                                                                376

                                                                                                Who wrote the book ‘Hujjat Allah Al Baligha’?

                                                                                                Correct Answer: Shah Wali Ullah
                                                                                                Show explanation
                                                                                                Explanation

                                                                                                The Conclusive Argument From God (Hujjat Allah al Baligha)

                                                                                                The Conclusive Argument of God is the masterwork of Shah Wallah of Delhi (1762)

                                                                                                Originally published on: 1979

                                                                                                Author of Hujjat Allah al Baligha: Shah Waliullah Dehlawi

                                                                                                Editor of Hujjat Allah al Baligha : Marcia Hermansen

                                                                                                ****

                                                                                                nd9-2-2023



                                                                                                377

                                                                                                Who is the Current Governor of Punjab?

                                                                                                Correct Answer: Sardar Saleem Haider
                                                                                                Show explanation
                                                                                                Explanation

                                                                                                Sardar Saleem Haider is the current and 37th Governor Punjab

                                                                                                Appointment date: 10 May 2024

                                                                                                Party name: PPP

                                                                                                Predecessor: Baligh ur Rehman 

                                                                                                Last Verified and Updated on 11-03-2026

                                                                                                Refrence Click here

                                                                                                ان سوالات کو ضرور پڑھیں

                                                                                                ·        Current Governor KPK

                                                                                                ·         Current Governor of Sindh

                                                                                                ·        Current Governor of Punjab

                                                                                                ·        Current Governor of Balochistan

                                                                                                378

                                                                                                Where is Katas Raj temple (Qila Katas) located?

                                                                                                Correct Answer: Chakwal
                                                                                                Show explanation
                                                                                                Explanation
                                                                                                • The Shri Katas Raj Temples is also known as Qila Katas.
                                                                                                • The temple is located in Choa Saidanshah, Chakwal, near the M2 Motorway.
                                                                                                • The temples were established by the Hindu Shahis between the mid-7th and 10th centuries.

                                                                                                379

                                                                                                "Aab e Hayat " was written by?

                                                                                                Correct Answer: Muhammad Hussain Azad
                                                                                                Show explanation
                                                                                                Explanation

                                                                                                Muhammad Husain Azad was a scholar and an Urdu writer.

                                                                                                who wrote both prose and poetry.

                                                                                                His best known work is Aab-e-Hayat. 

                                                                                                Born: May 5, 1830, Delhi, India

                                                                                                Died: January 22, 1910, Lahore

                                                                                                380

                                                                                                The length of Siachen glacier is _____?

                                                                                                Correct Answer: 47 miles
                                                                                                Show explanation
                                                                                                Explanation

                                                                                                The Siachen Glacier is approximately 76 km long, which is equal to 47 miles.

                                                                                                It is located in the Karakoram Range of the Himalayas.

                                                                                                It is the highest battlefield in the world.

                                                                                                The glacier is strategically important between India and Pakistan.

                                                                                                The glacier lies at an altitude of 17,000 to 22,000 feet.

                                                                                                Siachen is called the “Third Pole” due to its extensive ice coverage.

                                                                                                381

                                                                                                Where is lake Kachura situated in Pakistan?

                                                                                                Correct Answer: Skardu
                                                                                                Show explanation
                                                                                                Explanation

                                                                                                The Kachura Lakes are three lakes in the Skardu, Gilgit Baltistan.

                                                                                                The lakes, at 2,500 metres in elevation.

                                                                                                They are Upper Kachura Lake and Lower Kachura Lake.

                                                                                                382

                                                                                                Jallianwala Bagh is located in _____?

                                                                                                Correct Answer: Ameristar
                                                                                                Show explanation
                                                                                                Explanation

                                                                                                Jallianwala Bagh Massacre took place on 13 April 1919

                                                                                                In Jallianwala Bagh massacre killing at least 379 people and injuring over 1,200 other people.

                                                                                                Jallianwala Bagh is a historic garden close to the Golden Temple complex in Amritsar, Punjab, India

                                                                                                By General Reginald Dyer order did the massacre take place in Jallianwala Bagh

                                                                                                 It is managed by the Jallianwala Bagh National Memorial Trust and was renovated between 2019 and 2021****

                                                                                                nd9-2-2023


                                                                                                383

                                                                                                Jallianwala Bagh Massacre took place on?

                                                                                                Correct Answer: 13 April 1919
                                                                                                Show explanation
                                                                                                Explanation

                                                                                                The Jallianwala Bagh massacre, also known as the Amritsar massacre

                                                                                                Jallianwala Bagh Massacre took place on 13 April 1919

                                                                                                In Jallianwala Bagh massacre killing at least 379 people and injured over 1,200 other people.

                                                                                                Jallianwala Bagh is in Amritsar  Punjab, India

                                                                                                By General Reginald Dyer's order did the massacre take place in Jallianwala Bagh

                                                                                                NOW

                                                                                                Jallianwala Bagh is managed by the Jallianwala Bagh National Memorial Trust and was renovated between 2019 and 2021


                                                                                                ****

                                                                                                ND16-3-2023

                                                                                                384

                                                                                                Which cities of Iran and Pakistan are linked through railway line?

                                                                                                Correct Answer: Quetta-Zedan
                                                                                                Show explanation
                                                                                                Explanation

                                                                                                Pakistan and Iran are linked by the railway line connecting Quetta (Pakistan) to Zahedan (Iran)

                                                                                                Pakistan: The line runs from Quetta through Taftan to the Iranian border. 

                                                                                                Iran: The line continues from Mirjaveh (border) to Zahedan, connecting to the Iranian network. 

                                                                                                385

                                                                                                Doctrine of necessity was used in the case for the first time in the history of Pakistan?

                                                                                                Correct Answer: Maulvi Tamiz-ud-Din Vs State case
                                                                                                Show explanation
                                                                                                Explanation

                                                                                                The Doctrine of Necessity was first used in Maulvi Tamiz-ud-Din vs State (1955).

                                                                                                It was applied to validate the action of dissolving the Constituent Assembly of Pakistan.



                                                                                                386

                                                                                                Who was Pakistan 1st ambassador to the United Nations Organizations ?

                                                                                                Correct Answer: Patris bukhari
                                                                                                Show explanation
                                                                                                Explanation

                                                                                                Dr. Zafarullah Khan was Pakistan’s first ambassador to the United Nations in 1947.

                                                                                                He played a key role in representing Pakistan at the UN after independence.

                                                                                                He later served as the President of the UN General Assembly in 1962.

                                                                                                He was also Pakistan’s Foreign Minister from 1954 to 1955.

                                                                                                His efforts helped establish Pakistan’s early diplomatic presence internationally.

                                                                                                **

                                                                                                Pakistan Ambassador List 

                                                                                                On July 31, 2020, Mr. Moin ul Haque is the current ambassador of Pakistan to China 

                                                                                                Mansoor Khan has been the Current Ambassador of Pakistan to Afghanistan since 2020.

                                                                                                Lt. Gen. Bilal Akbar is the new ambassador of Pakistan to Saudi Arabia

                                                                                                Ambassador Zahoor Ahmedis the current Ambassador of Pakistan to the UK 2022

                                                                                                Mr. Ahmed Amjad Ali, Ambassador of Pakistan in Iraq

                                                                                                Ambassador Akram is the current ambassador of Pakistan to the UN

                                                                                                The current Pakistani Ambassador to Afghanistan is Obaid ur Rehman Nizamani.

                                                                                                ***



                                                                                                387

                                                                                                Al-Hilal weekly was started by____________?

                                                                                                Correct Answer: Abul Kalam Azad
                                                                                                Show explanation
                                                                                                Explanation

                                                                                                Al-Hilal in 1912 was Founder Maulana Abul Kalam Azad.

                                                                                                Launched: July 13, 1912.
                                                                                                Language: Urdu.

                                                                                                The newspaper was published in Urdu from Calcutta. 

                                                                                                Significance: It brought a new era to Urdu journalism with its anti-British stance and support for Indian self-rule.
                                                                                                Closure: Banned by the British government in 1914 under the Press Act.
                                                                                                388

                                                                                                Pakistan State Bank started working on 1st July 1948 with the help of Rs?

                                                                                                Correct Answer: Thirty Millions
                                                                                                Show explanation
                                                                                                Explanation

                                                                                                Pakistan State Bank started working on 1st July 1948 with the amount of Rs: Thirty Millions.

                                                                                                History of State Bank

                                                                                                It was inaugurated on 1st July 1948.

                                                                                                The headquarters is in Karachi, Sindh

                                                                                                Zahid Husain was the founder and the first governor of the SBP from June 1948 to July 1953.

                                                                                                Dr. Shamshad Akhtar was the first female Governor of the SBP in 2006.

                                                                                                Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah inaugurated the State Bank of Pakistan.

                                                                                                The Governor is appointed by the President of Pakistan.

                                                                                                Term of five years.

                                                                                                389

                                                                                                What is the main cause of failure of Pakistan India Iran gas pipeline project?

                                                                                                Correct Answer: Economic and political pressure of USA
                                                                                                Show explanation
                                                                                                Explanation

                                                                                                The Pakistan-India-Iran gas pipeline project was mainly failed due to economic and political pressure from the USA.

                                                                                                The project was officially cancelled around 2016.

                                                                                                390

                                                                                                Who is the author of the book Struggle For Pakistan"?

                                                                                                Correct Answer: I.H. Qureshi
                                                                                                Show explanation
                                                                                                Explanation

                                                                                                'I.H. Qureshi is the author of the book "struggle of Pakistan"

                                                                                                **

                                                                                                Ayesha Jalal wrote the book titled: The Struggle for Pakistan:

                                                                                                while I.H. Qureshi wrote the book: The Struggle for Pakistan

                                                                                                 Publish Date. 1965

                                                                                                ****

                                                                                                  ND 05-12-2022

                                                                                                  ***

                                                                                                  ND16-1-2023

                                                                                                  391

                                                                                                  Which of the following king's tomb is not situated in sub continent?

                                                                                                  Correct Answer: Babar
                                                                                                  Show explanation
                                                                                                  Explanation

                                                                                                  The tomb of Babur is located in the Gardens of Babur in Kabul, Afghanistan - (Not part of Indian Subcontinent)  

                                                                                                  - Jahangir – Tomb in Lahore, Pakistan (Subcontinent)  

                                                                                                  - Akbar – Tomb in Sikandra, Agra, India (Subcontinent)  

                                                                                                  - Aurangzeb – Tomb in Khuldabad, Maharashtra, India (Subcontinent)

                                                                                                  392

                                                                                                  Where Akhori Dam is located?

                                                                                                  Correct Answer: Punjab
                                                                                                  Show explanation
                                                                                                  Explanation

                                                                                                  Akhori Dam is located near Akhori Village in Attock District of Punjab.

                                                                                                  Situated on Nandna Kas, a tributary of the Haro River.

                                                                                                  Purpose: Water storage, hydropower generation, and flood control. 

                                                                                                  It has been proposed by the former Pakistan Muslim League (Q)'s Government.

                                                                                                  Developed by WAPDA as part of its Water Vision 2025, 

                                                                                                  It will also host a 600 MW hydropower plant

                                                                                                  Length 5.16 km

                                                                                                  393

                                                                                                  Who is the writer of the book "India wins freedom"?

                                                                                                  Correct Answer: Abul Kalam Azad
                                                                                                  Show explanation
                                                                                                  Explanation

                                                                                                  Molana Abul Kalam Azad was the author of  "The book India Wins Freedom". 

                                                                                                  India Wins Freedom was published on 1 January 1988.

                                                                                                  It was the introduction of the Indian Act of 1935 and different events of the subcontinent.

                                                                                                  394

                                                                                                  Who was the 1st CM of the Punjab?
